clean_body: (Trump’s cutting the TSA nonsense) | | | | | | | | Hey Friend, Ever stood in the airport security line without shoes, a belt, or even a shred of dignity—wondering if your orange Vols socks are about to trigger a college football showdown? Yeah, me too. For nearly 20 years, we’ve all been forced to take off our shoes and shuffle around like cattle while TSA agents bark orders. But in a miraculous turn of events, the rules are . Common sense in Washington? Rare. Common sense from the TSA? That’s like seeing pigs fly by. But to give credit where it's due, this magic moment was brought to us by the . The no-shoes rule did little to nothing for our safety. And let’s not forget—TSA lost all credibility when agents started spying on under Biden’s “Quiet Skies” program. The TSA’s priorities have been for a long time, targeting political opponents while turning a blind eye to thousands of migrants . Our own law-abiding citizens have been forced to jump through hoops while they let unvetted people breeze through checkpoints all over the dadgum country. I’m grateful that President Trump and his cabinet finally put an end to this nonsense, and I have a feeling you are too. Sincerely, Tim Burchett | | Join the conservative movement! Chip in to help us pave the way for a solid Republican agenda. | | | Stay in Touch 👇 Paid for by Burchett for Congress | | | ---|---|---

name: Texas Organizing Project
subject: Texans look out for each other and step up for each other / Los tejanos se cuidan y se intensifican el uno al otro
body:
Lea el boletín en español abajo [#Español]
Grieving with all impacted by last week's deadly flash floods in Central Texas
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Central%20Texas%20flood%20vigil%202025.jpg]
TOP is heartbroken by the devastation caused by flash flooding in the Texas Hill Country last week. We grieve for the lives lost as well as all families, friends, and fellow community members impacted by this catastrophic event.
The death toll has now surpassed 120, with dozens more still reported as missing as rescue crews continue to search for survivors.
As Texans, we look out for one another, and as a community organizing nonprofit committed to justice and liberation, we understand fully how direct assistance and access to vital resources in the aftermath of a climate disaster is critical — no matter your age, race, gender, faith, or income. Please share the following helpful links with your networks:
- Here's where you can donate to help Central Texas flood recovery [https://www.texastribune.org/2025/07/05/texas-hill-country-flooding-how-to-help/]
- Hill Country flooding: Here’s how to give and receive help [https://www.cbsnews.com/texas/news/where-to-donate-help-central-texas-flood-recovery/]
- Hands Off Central TX: July 4 flood mutual aid [https://www.handsoffcentraltx.org/]
In the days and weeks to come, all people who call our state home deserve honest answers from our local, state, and federal leaders in the wake of this tragedy , as well as an assessment of factors that contributed to this disaster and what can be done to mitigate the deadliness of such events in the future. Here is a quick explainer. [https://blog.ucs.org/rachel-cleetus/seven-things-you-need-to-know-about-the-texas-flash-flood-tragedy/]
TONIGHT IN DALLAS:
Community dinner info session on local Inflation Reduction Act funds
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Brighter%20Dallas%20community%20dinner%20flyer.PNG]
Dallas County is getting millions of dollars from the federal government to help lower energy bills, fix homes, and create good-paying jobs as a result of President Biden's Inflation Reduction Act (IRA). But if we don’t speak up, that money could go to wealthy areas and big developers — not our communities. We’re organizing to make sure Black, Latino, and working-class families in Dallas get the support we deserve.
Join TOP and members with the Brighter Dallas coalition TONIGHT, July 10, from 5:30 - 7:30 p.m. at the Beckley-Saner Rec. Center in Dallas for a family-friendly community event where we'll discuss how these IRA (Inflation Reduction Act) funds can benefit residents across Dallas County for years to come.
There’s still time to RSVP at toptx.us/ourmoney. [https://secure.everyaction.com/r5AdrDRdBEGm0Vv-N93R_w2]
For more information about this event and our IRA campaign in Dallas, contact Iman at ibouzid@organizetexas.org [ibouzid@organizetexas.org] .
Brainstorming for a better tomorrow after redistricting in Tarrant County
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/BlackTOP%20Tarrant%20County%20redistricting%20event.JPG]
With a 3-2 vote, the GOP-controlled Tarrant County Commissioners Court in June approved a redistricting map that strips Black and Latino communities of their political voice and power. [https://www.cbsnews.com/texas/news/tarrant-county-redistricting-approved-amid-public-protests-and-partisan-debates/] This mid-decade redraw, with no new census data, is an act of racial gerrymandering. It’s unethical and illegal.
This past Saturday afternoon at The Chef's Corner in Fort Worth, BlackTOP came together to break bread with community members to talk about how we can collectively fight against this political power grab and move our neighborhoods forward. We appreciate Tarrant County Commissioner Alisa Simmons of Precinct 2 for joining us!
The Voting Rights Act and the 14th Amendment were created to stop exactly this kind of racial discrimination. We won’t sit back while our communities are carved up and silenced. We will continue to fight for fair representation, for our voices to be heard, and for a Texas that truly represents all its people.
To learn more about our fight against this gerrymandering in Tarrant County and how you can join, contact David at dvillalobos@organizetexas.org [dvillalobos@organizetexas.org] .
TOP Tip: Fight authoritarianism -- read banned books this summer
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Fist%20air%20banned%20books.jpeg]
Try as they might, Trump and MAGA extremists will never erase our identities, our dreams, or the invaluable impact our communities' stories have had (and continue to have) on society. We're not allowing censorship in the name of white supremacy to stop our stories from being told and celebrated. Browse through this list of credible websites where you can read recently banned books for free. [https://mashable.com/article/read-banned-books-access]
Español
De luto con todos los afectados por las inundaciones repentinas mortales de la semana pasada en el centro de Texas
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Central%20Texas%20flood%20vigil%202025.jpg]
TOP está desconsolado por la devastación causada por las inundaciones repentinas en Texas Hill County la semana pasada. Lamentamos las vidas perdidas, así como a todas las familias, amigos y miembros de la comunidad afectados por este catastrófico evento.
El número de muertos ya supera los 120, y decenas más siguen desaparecidos mientras los equipos de rescate continúan buscando sobrevivientes.
Como tejanos, nos cuidamos mutuamente, y como organización comunitaria sin fines de lucro comprometida con la justicia y la liberación, comprendemos plenamente la importancia de la asistencia directa y el acceso a recursos vitales tras un desastre climático, sin importar la edad, raza, género, religión o ingresos. Comparta los siguientes enlaces útiles con sus redes:
- Aquí puede donar para ayudar a la recuperación de las inundaciones en el centro de Texas [https://www.texastribune.org/2025/07/05/texas-hill-country-flooding-how-to-help/]
- Inundaciones en Hill Country: Cómo dar y recibir ayuda [https://www.cbsnews.com/texas/news/where-to-donate-help-central-texas-flood-recovery/]
- Hands Off Central TX: Ayuda mutua por las inundaciones del 4 de julio [https://www.handsoffcentraltx.org/]
En los próximos días y semanas, todos los que consideran nuestro estado su hogar merecen respuestas honestas de nuestros líderes locales, estatales y federales tras esta tragedia , así como una evaluación de los factores que contribuyeron a este desastre y qué se puede hacer para mitigar la letalidad de tales eventos en el futuro. Aquí tiene una breve explicación [https://blog.ucs.org/rachel-cleetus/seven-things-you-need-to-know-about-the-texas-flash-flood-tragedy/] . [https://blog.ucs.org/rachel-cleetus/seven-things-you-need-to-know-about-the-texas-flash-flood-tragedy/]
ESTA NOCHE EN DALLAS:
Cena comunitaria con información sobre los fondos locales de la Ley de Reducción de la Inflación
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Brighter%20Dallas%20community%20dinner%20flyer.PNG]
El condado de Dallas está recibiendo millones de dólares del gobierno federal para ayudar a reducir las facturas de energía, reparar hogares y crear empleos bien remunerados gracias a la Ley de Reducción de la Inflación (IRA) del presidente Biden. Pero si no alzamos la voz, ese dinero podría ir a áreas ricas y grandes desarrolladores, y no a nuestras comunidades. Nos estamos organizando para asegurarnos de que las familias morenas, latinas y trabajadoras en Dallas reciban el apoyo que merecen.
Únete a TOP y miembros de la coalición Brighter Dallas HOY, 10 de julio, de 5:30 a 7:30 p.m. en el Centro Recreativo Beckley-Saner en Dallas para un evento comunitario apto para familias donde discutiremos cómo estos fondos de la IRA pueden beneficiar a los residentes de todo el condado de Dallas durante años venideros.
Aún hay tiempo para confirmar tu asistencia en toptx.us/nuestro-dinero. [https://secure.everyaction.com/r5AdrDRdBEGm0Vv-N93R_w2]
Para más información sobre este evento y nuestra campaña de la IRA en Dallas, contacta a Iman en ibouzid@organizetexas.org [ibouzid@organizetexas.org] .
Lluvia de ideas para un futuro mejor tras la redistribución de distritos en el condado de Tarrant
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/BlackTOP%20Tarrant%20County%20redistricting%20event.JPG]
Con una votación de 3 a 2, la Corte de Comisionados del Condado de Tarrant, controlada por el Partido Republicano, aprobó en junio un mapa de redistribución de distritos que priva a las comunidades morenas y latinas de su voz y poder político. [https://www.cbsnews.com/texas/news/tarrant-county-redistricting-approved-amid-public-protests-and-partisan-debates/] Esta redistribución de distritos a mediados de la década, sin nuevos datos del censo, constituye un acto de manipulación racial de los distritos electorales. Es antiético e ilegal.
El sábado pasado por la tarde en The Chef's Corner en Fort Worth, BlackTOP se reunió para compartir pan con miembros de la comunidad para hablar sobre cómo podemos luchar colectivamente contra este intento de apoderarse del poder político y avanzar en nuestros barrios. ¡Agradecemos a la comisionada del condado de Tarrant, Alisa Simmons, del Precinto 2, por unirse a nosotros!
La Ley de Derecho al Voto y la Decimocuarta Enmienda se crearon precisamente para detener este tipo de discriminación racial. No nos quedaremos de brazos cruzados mientras nuestras comunidades son divididas y silenciadas. Seguiremos luchando por una representación justa, para que nuestras voces sean escuchadas y por un Texas que realmente represente a toda su gente.
Para obtener más información sobre nuestra lucha contra esta manipulación de los distritos electorales en el Condado de Tarrant y cómo unirse, contacte a David en dvillalobos@organizetexas.org [dvillalobos@organizetexas.org] .
Consejos de TOP: Lucha contra el autoritarismo -- lee libros prohibidos este verano
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/TSM/TSMTO/1/45640/images/Fist%20air%20banned%20books.jpeg]
Por mucho que lo intenten, Trump y los extremistas de MAGA nunca borrarán nuestras identidades, nuestros sueños ni el invaluable impacto que las historias de nuestras comunidades han tenido (y siguen teniendo) en la sociedad. No permitiremos que la censura en nombre de la supremacía blanca impida que nuestras historias se cuenten y celebren. Explora esta lista de sitios web confiables donde puedes leer libros gratis recientemente prohibidos. [https://mashable.com/article/read-banned-books-access]

clean_body: Lea el boletín en español abajo [#Español] Grieving with all impacted by last week's deadly flash floods in Central Texas [] TOP is heartbroken by the devastation caused by flash flooding in the Texas Hill Country last week. We grieve for the lives lost as well as all families, friends, and fellow community members impacted by this catastrophic event. The death toll has now surpassed 120, with dozens more still reported as missing as rescue crews continue to search for survivors. As Texans, we look out for one another, and as a community organizing nonprofit committed to justice and liberation, we understand fully how direct assistance and access to vital resources in the aftermath of a climate disaster is critical — no matter your age, race, gender, faith, or income. Please share the following helpful links with your networks: - Here's where you can donate to help Central Texas flood recovery [] - Hill Country flooding: Here’s how to give and receive help [] - Hands Off Central TX: July 4 flood mutual aid [] In the days and weeks to come, all people who call our state home deserve honest answers from our local, state, and federal leaders in the wake of this tragedy , as well as an assessment of factors that contributed to this disaster and what can be done to mitigate the deadliness of such events in the future. Here is a quick explainer. [] TONIGHT IN DALLAS: Community dinner info session on local Inflation Reduction Act funds [] Dallas County is getting millions of dollars from the federal government to help lower energy bills, fix homes, and create good-paying jobs as a result of President Biden's Inflation Reduction Act (IRA). But if we don’t speak up, that money could go to wealthy areas and big developers — not our communities. We’re organizing to make sure Black, Latino, and working-class families in Dallas get the support we deserve. Join TOP and members with the Brighter Dallas coalition TONIGHT, July 10, from 5:30 - 7:30 p.m. at the Beckley-Saner Rec. Center in Dallas for a family-friendly community event where we'll discuss how these IRA (Inflation Reduction Act) funds can benefit residents across Dallas County for years to come. There’s still time to RSVP at toptx.us/ourmoney. [] For more information about this event and our IRA campaign in Dallas, contact Iman at ibouzid@organizetexas.org [ibouzid@organizetexas.org] . Brainstorming for a better tomorrow after redistricting in Tarrant County [] With a 3-2 vote, the GOP-controlled Tarrant County Commissioners Court in June approved a redistricting map that strips Black and Latino communities of their political voice and power. [] This mid-decade redraw, with no new census data, is an act of racial gerrymandering. It’s unethical and illegal. This past Saturday afternoon at The Chef's Corner in Fort Worth, BlackTOP came together to break bread with community members to talk about how we can collectively fight against this political power grab and move our neighborhoods forward. We appreciate Tarrant County Commissioner Alisa Simmons of Precinct 2 for joining us! The Voting Rights Act and the 14th Amendment were created to stop exactly this kind of racial discrimination. We won’t sit back while our communities are carved up and silenced. We will continue to fight for fair representation, for our voices to be heard, and for a Texas that truly represents all its people. To learn more about our fight against this gerrymandering in Tarrant County and how you can join, contact David at dvillalobos@organizetexas.org [dvillalobos@organizetexas.org] . TOP Tip: Fight authoritarianism -- read banned books this summer [] Try as they might, Trump and MAGA extremists will never erase our identities, our dreams, or the invaluable impact our communities' stories have had (and continue to have) on society. We're not allowing censorship in the name of white supremacy to stop our stories from being told and celebrated. Browse through this list of credible websites where you can read recently banned books for free. [] Español De luto con todos los afectados por las inundaciones repentinas mortales de la semana pasada en el centro de Texas [] TOP está desconsolado por la devastación causada por las inundaciones repentinas en Texas Hill County la semana pasada. Lamentamos las vidas perdidas, así como a todas las familias, amigos y miembros de la comunidad afectados por este catastrófico evento. El número de muertos ya supera los 120, y decenas más siguen desaparecidos mientras los equipos de rescate continúan buscando sobrevivientes. Como tejanos, nos cuidamos mutuamente, y como organización comunitaria sin fines de lucro comprometida con la justicia y la liberación, comprendemos plenamente la importancia de la asistencia directa y el acceso a recursos vitales tras un desastre climático, sin importar la edad, raza, género, religión o ingresos. Comparta los siguientes enlaces útiles con sus redes: - Aquí puede donar para ayudar a la recuperación de las inundaciones en el centro de Texas [] - Inundaciones en Hill Country: Cómo dar y recibir ayuda [] - Hands Off Central TX: Ayuda mutua por las inundaciones del 4 de julio [] En los próximos días y semanas, todos los que consideran nuestro estado su hogar merecen respuestas honestas de nuestros líderes locales, estatales y federales tras esta tragedia , así como una evaluación de los factores que contribuyeron a este desastre y qué se puede hacer para mitigar la letalidad de tales eventos en el futuro. Aquí tiene una breve explicación [] . # [Anti-Muslim Hate Is Taking Its Toll](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=167957741&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3OTU3NzQxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTIxNTk4NjUsImV4cCI6MTc1NDc1MTg2NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Xs85qJ-1DuV7rWA4uN_G1Edm-T6L244MGpD-kGLaIy0)
### The impact of normalized Islamophobia on millions of Americans--and why silence is not an option
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jul 10
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=167957741&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3OTU3NzQxLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MjE1OTg2NSwiZXhwIjoxNzU0NzUxODY1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.oHIqpaM8cYCH3NnRxzBqvcdLw8MzvSEnFmkc9PtILSk&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=167957741&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3OTU3NzQxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTIxNTk4NjUsImV4cCI6MTc1NDc1MTg2NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Xs85qJ-1DuV7rWA4uN_G1Edm-T6L244MGpD-kGLaIy0&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=167957741&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3OTU3NzQxLCJpYXQiOjE3NTIxNTk4NjUsImV4cCI6MTc1NDc1MTg2NSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Xs85qJ-1DuV7rWA4uN_G1Edm-T6L244MGpD-kGLaIy0)
---
| | [](https://substack..WgkbHY1LHTcTAEa6RKHXzN17dyK76cdwREVVvUaArnY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/anti-muslim-hate-is-taking-its-toll?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

There are 8760 hours in a year. In 2024 there were a record 8,658 anti-Muslim discrimination complaints.
That's essentially one act of anti-Muslim discrimination for every hour of every day for all 365 days of the year. It's the highest ever recorded since CAIR began gathering this data in 1996--even higher than in the year after 9/11. What we're seeing unfold isn't just a political attack on a single candidate or community--it's an all-out campaign to normalize anti-Muslim bigotry in America. And it's working. Such dehumanizing hate ultimately leads to violence against minority communities. And I'm guessing this is probably the first you've heard about how severe this crisis has become. **Let 's Address This.**
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..usi7RPMOk0snc_tv8HwwhEPYIp1CUmyWbkgBPuVj8bQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=167957741)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/43a435c3-988b-4d3c-ac42-ef8ab336d9f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
## The Real Threat: Not Fascism, But a Muslim for Mayor?
Across media, politics, and tech, the message is consistent: a Muslim who challenges the system is a threat, but a racist who maintains it is a leader. This isn't just harming the 4-6 million Americans who are Muslim. It's devastating entire generations--young people who are watching in real-time as bigotry is not only tolerated but rewarded.
Corporate media has been more outraged at the _possibility_ of a Democratic Socialist like Zohran Mamdani entering New York's Mayor's office than at the _certainty_ that a white supremacist has returned to the White House. The same outlets that condemn Mamdani over things he never even said meanwhile shrug at Donald Trump's open racism that he definitely repeats. Trump, meanwhile, has taken to his national platform to condemn Mamdani at every turn, even threatening to arrest and deport him.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/242040e4-268d-495c-9778-c2b9c4eb256f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..usi7RPMOk0snc_tv8HwwhEPYIp1CUmyWbkgBPuVj8bQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=167957741)
## Silicon Valley Welcomes Racists
In Big Tech, the hypocrisy is just as grotesque. Elon Musk--a formerly undocumented white immigrant--[can openly dehumanize Muslims](https://substack.com/redirect/ad668feb-bbd2-4821-987c-d90a54f6d9da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), spread dangerous disinformation, and face zero accountability. Likewise, VC Shaun Maguire has gone on a lengthy sustained tirade of hate against American Muslims by claiming, among other things, that "[lying is a virtue in Muslim culture](https://substack.com/redirect/06027be6-cd70-4c0f-986c-4cf86100165a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." He has faced zero consequences for his xenophobia and hate. On the contrary, an open letter from fellow Silicon Valley VCs, CEOs, and entrepreneurs actually _backs_ Maguire's racist and Islamophobic comments, [declaring](https://substack.com/redirect/3657b8a7-dd1a-4db1-9ce7-4a9a09f93d10?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> [Shauns'] words were not hate speech - they were the reflections of a principled thinker and a partner to countless founders who span geographies, faiths, and political beliefs.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e9e3aa6b-0019-4cde-b5c8-ed7b377c5018?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
While an earlier letter called for Maguire to apologize and to face consequences for his hate, this subsequent letter has already put that to rest. This isn't just a double standard. It's a roadmap for fascism.
Republicans, as expected, are going full throttle with anti-Muslim hate. They have long called us terrorists, demanded loyalty tests, and even pushed legislation to monitor, ban, and silence Muslim communities. This recent tweet by MAGA racist Congressman Randy Fine, directed at Congresswoman Ilhan Omar, exemplifies the point above.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/effbdeeb-7f12-4d17-bed7-713c9acf636c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
But let's not pretend this is only a GOP issue. Democrats are sadly joining in. And that's perhaps the most devastating part about. There's very little that Republicans have said to dehumanize American Muslims, that some Democratic Party leaders have not also said (and faced no consequences thereof). For example, corporate Democrats like Hakeem Jeffries and Chuck Schumer are following a similar playbook: demand that Mamdani denounce statements he never made, while ignoring the violent, racist, and anti-Muslim rhetoric Trump shouts from every podium.
Even more disturbing, Senator Kirsten Gillibrand accused Zohran Mamdani of supporting "global jihad" and terrorism--an utterly baseless and reckless smear. She doubled and then tripled down in the same interview, refusing to acknowledge that she was lying and spreading disinformation.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5e28ce71-f30b-4e6f-b92f-f8df68e23f9c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
After immense time and pressure--effort that could have been used to combat the Big Ugly Bill--she reportedly apologized to Zohran Mamdani in private. As of the writing of this piece she has refused to issue a public apology. And make no mistake: if the bigotry was public, the apology must be, too. Anything less is a refusal to acknowledge the harm already inflicted on millions of Muslims watching this unfold. Yet, that is the reality we face right now.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..usi7RPMOk0snc_tv8HwwhEPYIp1CUmyWbkgBPuVj8bQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=167957741)
## Media Institutions That Back Hate
Meanwhile, Democratic candidate Whitney Tilson--the long shot NYC mayoral candidate who smeared Mamdani with vile Islamophobic ads falsely accusing him of wanting to kill Jews--was tacitly _endorsed_ by the _New York Times._ What message does that send to Muslim Americans about our place in this country?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8501d120-6b12-4387-a0de-33fd5a83d06e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
While corporate media enabled hate, I must take a moment to recognize the courageous voice of Jewish New Yorkers, who loudly condemned Tilson's anti-Muslim and Islamophobic bigotry. The fact is that antisemitism and Islamophobia are two sides of the same coin. It is a noble testament of the power of unity against hate, to see Jewish and Muslim New Yorkers stand united against Tilson's bigotry.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..usi7RPMOk0snc_tv8HwwhEPYIp1CUmyWbkgBPuVj8bQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=167957741)
## The Real Toll: Violence, Silence, and Trauma
Let's not kid ourselves. This isn't just about political reputations or campaign narratives. When elected officials and national outlets spread lies about Muslims, they invite real violence into our communities. Hate speech leads to hate crimes. Dehumanization leads to murder. And the psychological toll on Muslim children--on our daughters, our sons, our students, our neighbors--is immeasurable.
They're watching. They're internalizing. And unless we act, they will carry these scars for life. And it isn't just Muslim children. All children are watching and seeing the normalization of hate, and learning how to hate. This is why we cannot be silent, and why we must continue to loudly speak up against such hate.
I am grateful for this community that fights back. That sees through the lies. That refuses to let racism, Islamophobia, or authoritarianism define who we are or what our future holds. If you're reading this, then you are part of that movement. And I'm asking you to stay engaged. Continue to speak out--in your schools, your churches, your synagogues, your temples, your mosques. Continue to engage with this content and let me know how I can better arm you with the knowledge to debunk anti-Muslim and Islamophobic hate. And if you haven't already, [subscribe either as a free or paid subscriber](https://substack.com/redirect/98617f82-d6b9-437e-9f5d-d45f447d3055?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). clean_body: The impact of normalized Islamophobia on millions of Americans--and why silence is not an option | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Thank you for being an awesome subscriber! When possible, I hope you'll consider upgrading to a $6/mo or $50/year subscription! The impact of normalized Islamophobia on millions of Americans--and why silence is not an option | | | Jul 10 | | | | | | | | | | | | There are 8760 hours in a year. In 2024 there were a record 8,658 anti-Muslim discrimination complaints. That's essentially one act of anti-Muslim discrimination for every hour of every day for all 365 days of the year. It's the highest ever recorded since CAIR began gathering this data in 1996--even higher than in the year after 9/11. What we're seeing unfold isn't just a political attack on a single candidate or community--it's an all-out campaign to normalize anti-Muslim bigotry in America. And it's working. Such dehumanizing hate ultimately leads to violence against minority communities. And I'm guessing this is probably the first you've heard about how severe this crisis has become. Let 's Address This. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. | | ---|---|--- The Real Threat: Not Fascism, But a Muslim for Mayor? Across media, politics, and tech, the message is consistent: a Muslim who challenges the system is a threat, but a racist who maintains it is a leader. This isn't just harming the 4-6 million Americans who are Muslim. It's devastating entire generations--young people who are watching in real-time as bigotry is not only tolerated but rewarded. Corporate media has been more outraged at the possibility of a Democratic Socialist like Zohran Mamdani entering New York's Mayor's office than at the certainty that a white supremacist has returned to the White House. The same outlets that condemn Mamdani over things he never even said meanwhile shrug at Donald Trump's open racism that he definitely repeats. Trump, meanwhile, has taken to his national platform to condemn Mamdani at every turn, even threatening to arrest and deport him. | | ---|---|--- Silicon Valley Welcomes Racists In Big Tech, the hypocrisy is just as grotesque. Elon Musk--a formerly undocumented white immigrant--, spread dangerous disinformation, and face zero accountability. Likewise, VC Shaun Maguire has gone on a lengthy sustained tirade of hate against American Muslims by claiming, among other things, that "." He has faced zero consequences for his xenophobia and hate. On the contrary, an open letter from fellow Silicon Valley VCs, CEOs, and entrepreneurs actually backs Maguire's racist and Islamophobic comments, : [Shauns'] words were not hate speech - they were the reflections of a principled thinker and a partner to countless founders who span geographies, faiths, and political beliefs. | | ---|---|--- While an earlier letter called for Maguire to apologize and to face consequences for his hate, this subsequent letter has already put that to rest. This isn't just a double standard. It's a roadmap for fascism. Republicans, as expected, are going full throttle with anti-Muslim hate. They have long called us terrorists, demanded loyalty tests, and even pushed legislation to monitor, ban, and silence Muslim communities. This recent tweet by MAGA racist Congressman Randy Fine, directed at Congresswoman Ilhan Omar, exemplifies the point above. | | ---|---|--- But let's not pretend this is only a GOP issue. Democrats are sadly joining in. And that's perhaps the most devastating part about. There's very little that Republicans have said to dehumanize American Muslims, that some Democratic Party leaders have not also said (and faced no consequences thereof). For example, corporate Democrats like Hakeem Jeffries and Chuck Schumer are following a similar playbook: demand that Mamdani denounce statements he never made, while ignoring the violent, racist, and anti-Muslim rhetoric Trump shouts from every podium. Even more disturbing, Senator Kirsten Gillibrand accused Zohran Mamdani of supporting "global jihad" and terrorism--an utterly baseless and reckless smear. She doubled and then tripled down in the same interview, refusing to acknowledge that she was lying and spreading disinformation. | | ---|---|--- After immense time and pressure--effort that could have been used to combat the Big Ugly Bill--she reportedly apologized to Zohran Mamdani in private. As of the writing of this piece she has refused to issue a public apology. And make no mistake: if the bigotry was public, the apology must be, too. Anything less is a refusal to acknowledge the harm already inflicted on millions of Muslims watching this unfold. Yet, that is the reality we face right now. Media Institutions That Back Hate Meanwhile, Democratic candidate Whitney Tilson--the long shot NYC mayoral candidate who smeared Mamdani with vile Islamophobic ads falsely accusing him of wanting to kill Jews--was tacitly endorsed by the New York Times. What message does that send to Muslim Americans about our place in this country? | | ---|---|--- While corporate media enabled hate, I must take a moment to recognize the courageous voice of Jewish New Yorkers, who loudly condemned Tilson's anti-Muslim and Islamophobic bigotry. The fact is that antisemitism and Islamophobia are two sides of the same coin. It is a noble testament of the power of unity against hate, to see Jewish and Muslim New Yorkers stand united against Tilson's bigotry. The Real Toll: Violence, Silence, and Trauma Let's not kid ourselves. This isn't just about political reputations or campaign narratives. When elected officials and national outlets spread lies about Muslims, they invite real violence into our communities. Hate speech leads to hate crimes. Dehumanization leads to murder. And the psychological toll on Muslim children--on our daughters, our sons, our students, our neighbors--is immeasurable. They're watching. They're internalizing. And unless we act, they will carry these scars for life. And it isn't just Muslim children. All children are watching and seeing the normalization of hate, and learning how to hate. This is why we cannot be silent, and why we must continue to loudly speak up against such hate. I am grateful for this community that fights back. That sees through the lies. That refuses to let racism, Islamophobia, or authoritarianism define who we are or what our future holds. If you're reading this, then you are part of that movement. And I'm asking you to stay engaged. Continue to speak out--in your schools, your churches, your synagogues, your temples, your mosques. Continue to engage with this content and let me know how I can better arm you with the knowledge to debunk anti-Muslim and Islamophobic hate. And if you haven't already, . We will never have a paywall, but your support keeps this work going, especially as corporate media continues to fail. Last year we witnessed a record 8,658 anti-Muslim discrimination complaints. We have the power to drive that number down in 2025, but we must remain active against hate. We don't win this fight by playing it safe. We win by showing up. Loudly. Relentlessly. Truthfully. This seems like something out of a twisted novel, folks, but **the new top voting lawyer at the Department of Justice was until recently an attorney and activist for a leading anti-voting legal group** that has worked for years to spread fear about unproven illegal voting and press election officials to implement stricter, suppressive voting rules. The lawyer, Maureen Riordan, worked side-by-side with Cleta Mitchell — the right-wing activist who played a key role in President Donald Trump’s failed bid to subvert the results of the 2020 election — following Mitchell’s pledge to “ _reclaim our election systems from the left._ ” This appointment, along with the posting of Trump campaign lawyer and election denier Harmeet Dhillon as head of the Civil Rights division, shows how DOJ has gone from a largely consistent defender of voting rights to instead working actively to undermine our fundamental rights. **Again, are we surprised? No.** But these moves from the administration highlight how badly **we need to fight for our fundamental rights as citizens** , from the outside of government. **[Please, chip in $25 or whatever you can today to help us defend the right to vote across the country, from state legislatures to courtrooms to the ballot box>>](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111227278/556586615/1723810624?am=25.00&contactdata=qJTq2BsoxpoCY+ZX38+t7V2unoc+lc6n+9x+ysk5QnO7B6tbiGvsdN2brwrdPH2si/LXlEftrGBF3xMVUHHx8UONX5cA7KocNRlUPyLY6qg=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImRkYTUxN2RmLTllNWQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=mk096joHizs5hJDT0spP-CtvZuaFaYAIboV6vAqN0qs=&emci=74a8cbb0-995d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=dda517df-9e5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=33603084)**

| | Riordan's first lawsuit at her new job? **Filed recently against North Carolina, it cites Trump’s March anti-voter executive order and aims to strip legitimate voters from the rolls** and force North Carolina to do more to collect unnecessary information that’s required by new, suppressive state laws, all from people newly registering to vote. This follows her work at the harmless-sounding Public Interest Legal Foundation (PILF), which is anything but. PILF specializes in pressuring states and counties — _by threatening and bringing expensive and frivolous lawsuits_ — to more aggressively remove voters from the rolls. As part of that effort, PILF has worked to stoke fear about the threat of non-citizen voting, which is extremely rare. Remember, the civil rights division of DOJ was created in 1957 -- often referred to as the “ _crown jewel_ ” of the department -- has always been a cornerstone in the fight to protect our right to vote, not suing to restrict it or cut off access for voters seeking to exercise their rights. But Trump's nominees to DOJ have**sent out “mission statements” that made it clear the civil rights division was shifting its focus from protecting the civil rights of marginalized people to supporting Trump’s priorities and anti-voting conspiracy theories.** This administration is seemingly going to do everything in their power to attack our right to vote until they ensconce their control forever. clean_body: DemDocket: DOJ’s New Top Voting Lawyer Worked for Leading Anti-Voting Law Firm| | | | | This seems like something out of a twisted novel, folks, but the new top voting lawyer at the Department of Justice was until recently an attorney and activist for a leading anti-voting legal group that has worked for years to spread fear about unproven illegal voting and press election officials to implement stricter, suppressive voting rules. The lawyer, Maureen Riordan, worked side-by-side with Cleta Mitchell — the right-wing activist who played a key role in President Donald Trump’s failed bid to subvert the results of the 2020 election — following Mitchell’s pledge to “ reclaim our election systems from the left. ” This appointment, along with the posting of Trump campaign lawyer and election denier Harmeet Dhillon as head of the Civil Rights division, shows how DOJ has gone from a largely consistent defender of voting rights to instead working actively to undermine our fundamental rights. Again, are we surprised? No. But these moves from the administration highlight how badly we need to fight for our fundamental rights as citizens , from the outside of government. If you've saved your payment information with FastAction, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | | | | | | Riordan's first lawsuit at her new job? Filed recently against North Carolina, it cites Trump’s March anti-voter executive order and aims to strip legitimate voters from the rolls and force North Carolina to do more to collect unnecessary information that’s required by new, suppressive state laws, all from people newly registering to vote. This follows her work at the harmless-sounding Public Interest Legal Foundation (PILF), which is anything but. PILF specializes in pressuring states and counties — by threatening and bringing expensive and frivolous lawsuits — to more aggressively remove voters from the rolls. As part of that effort, PILF has worked to stoke fear about the threat of non-citizen voting, which is extremely rare. Remember, the civil rights division of DOJ was created in 1957 -- often referred to as the “ crown jewel ” of the department -- has always been a cornerstone in the fight to protect our right to vote, not suing to restrict it or cut off access for voters seeking to exercise their rights. But Trump's nominees to DOJ havesent out “mission statements” that made it clear the civil rights division was shifting its focus from protecting the civil rights of marginalized people to supporting Trump’s priorities and anti-voting conspiracy theories. This administration is seemingly going to do everything in their power to attack our right to vote until they ensconce their control forever. We have to keep fighting back, no matter what the cost. | | | | | | ---|---|---|--- Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country. Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. Fellow Conservative,

In Congress, I fight every day to bring Ohio values to Washington: strong communities, thriving businesses, and a government that works for YOU.

But I need your help to keep pushing forward.

**The radical left has put a massive target on my back, listing my seat as one of the top to flip Blue in 2026.**

Washington liberals are eager and prepared to pour millions into our district to push their out-of-touch agenda.

Fellow Conservative, we can’t let them drown out Ohio’s voice. Your support today will help us:

name: Heartland Patriots HQ
subject: Where do you get your news?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
Your input will help our movement move smarter and reach even more voters.
#
**Where do you get your news, Peter?**
**[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.lucaskunce.com/survey/hp-news-survey-july25?t=1&akid=9101%2E614464%2EGcCX1G)**
Every day, Americans choose to find their news from a variety of sources — social media platforms like Facebook and TikTok, podcasts, cable news networks, YouTube channels, print media, online publications like Substack, and more.
**If we want to break through the noise and reach voters where they are _right now,_ we need to know how they’re staying informed.** That’s why we’re asking for your help today.
**[Will you take just 90 seconds today to tell us more about where you find your news? Click here or use the button below to access our short survey.](https://act.lucaskunce.com/survey/hp-news-survey-july25?t=2&akid=9101%2E614464%2EGcCX1G)**
**[TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.lucaskunce.com/survey/hp-news-survey-july25?t=3&akid=9101%2E614464%2EGcCX1G)**
Your input will equip our movement with the information it needs to move smarter, reach even more voters, and uplift candidates working to improve the lives of working families across the Heartland.
Thank you in advance,
Heartland Patriots HQ

name: Team Baldwin
subject: Priorities Survey for Peter
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
| Share your thoughts:
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=4863%2E35263%2EeFnVwh)
Peter, Republicans in the Senate are **laser-focused** on delivering huge tax breaks for billionaires. They’re slashing health care for seniors and sick kids, ripping food assistance away from hungry families, and brushing off the impacts of their Big BS Bill with _“get over it”_ and _“we’re all going to die.”_ While Republicans side with the ultra-wealthy, Tammy and her Democratic colleagues are committed to fighting for working people, defending our healthcare, and cutting costs. But to do this work, Tammy needs to know what’s on your mind. The last thing she wants is your concerns or experiences to get lost in the endless shuffle of headlines. **[Peter, will you take our priorities survey today? We really value your response and it should only take a minute or two →](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/survey/Priorities_Survey_2025?refcode=AU_STB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250710_AM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=4863%2E35263%2EeFnVwh)** | | [TAKE THE SURVEY](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/survey/Priorities_Survey_2025?refcode=AU_STB_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250710_AM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=4863%2E35263%2EeFnVwh)

# Texas Agriculture Commission handles a nearly Billion dollar budget for the state that generates tremendous revenue and is strategic asset for not just Texas, but, our nation and the world.
---
| |
---
---
**Tomorrow, SID MILLER is our special guest at the BIZPAC Roundtable luncheon. Like always, we'll take a deep dive into the critical business of our Agriculture Commission and programs most do not even know about.** Commissioner Miller oversees entire industries for Texas including food crop production, livestock, weights and measures for fuels, rural economic redevelopment, nutritional school lunches, and is even helping with disaster relief from the Hill Country flooding tragedy. We'll learn about this and more and how our businesses can help and be involved in commerce with 200 rural counties that would welcome our involvement.
---
|
---
Our friend Tom Glass will be on deck with report on this legislature. I like to call it: "after the smoke clears" reporting or "what they just did to us".
Tom is a recognized and appreciated member of his community active in civic and legislative matters. Often time, Tom provides expert testimony and speaks up for small business owners in the legislature committee hearings.
Doors open at 11am and we will finish at 1 sharp. Do not miss this meeting and **bring guests.**
Houston Country Club
Potomac Room
11am to 1pm
Business Casual Dress Code
| | [ REGISTER NOW ](https://email.cloud.secureclick.net/c/33448?id=2374978.873.1.6e995a94fa02dc8dfaafce6558eb9f59)

name: California Dems
subject: Here’s our challenge
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5]
Have you been following our efforts to build up the regional office budget through our Forever Organizing Fund?
Sometimes our supporters will reach out to us and ask why we are fundraising for things like regional offices over a year before the election. Sometimes they will say they only like to donate a few weeks before an election.
And we get it . Politics can be exhausting in this era of Donald Trump, and fundraising isn’t particularly glamorous.
But, we’ve proven time and time again that we can defy the odds and win elections by mobilizing early. In 2024, we flipped three seats from red to blue because our Forever Organizing Program had regional offices up and running a year early (in 2023). Now, in 2025, we are working to build the same momentum for the midterms next year. Will you pitch in $ to help fund regional offices? We still need to raise another $3,999.50. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-july25?&refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-july25?&refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-july25?&refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-july25?&refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-july25?&refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-july25?&refcode=&amount=]Sometimes we also face another problem:
When we try to raise money for things like regional offices, field organizers, literature, and more, supporters will question why California is worth investing in.
A donor recently pointed out that Kamala Harris won the state decisively in 2024. So, why do we need regional offices?
Here’s the thing: While statewide we do tilt blue, there are more “tossup” and “lean” U.S. House districts in California than ANY other state.
That means that on Election Night, control of the U.S. House very often comes down to these purple seats in California. We were able to deliver three red to blue flips in 2024, but because Democrats lost seats in other states, we didn’t take back the U.S. House.
Now we are ready to finish the job by defending all our vulnerable incumbents and flipping more seats. But a key part of that strategy is opening up regional offices, and right now our budget is still $3,999.50 short. Will you pitch in a donation? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-july25?&refcode=&amount=]
CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-july25?&refcode=&amount=]Thank you,
- CADEM

DONATE » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=&amount=20.24]
Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action.

[=] CHIP IN $ >> [=] CHIP IN $ >> [=] CHIP IN $ >> [=] CHIP IN $ >> [=] CHIP IN $ >> [ we also face another problem: When we try to raise money for things like regional offices, field organizers, literature, and more, supporters will question why California is worth investing in. A donor recently pointed out that Kamala Harris won the state decisively in 2024. So, why do we need regional offices? Here’s the thing: While statewide we do tilt blue, there are more “tossup” and “lean” U.S. House districts in California than ANY other state. That means that on Election Night, control of the U.S. House very often comes down to these purple seats in California. We were able to deliver three red to blue flips in 2024, but because Democrats lost seats in other states, we didn’t take back the U.S. House. Now we are ready to finish the job by defending all our vulnerable incumbents and flipping more seats. But a key part of that strategy is opening up regional offices, and right now our budget is still $3,999.50 short. Will you pitch in a donation? [=] CHIP IN $ >> [ you, - CADEM DONATE » [] Our Democratic leaders are working hard to make California a better place to live. From fighting for good-paying jobs, combating climate change, to expanding healthcare access so all Californians can lead safe, healthy, prosperous lives — California’s Democratic leaders turn progressive ideas into meaningful action. If this email was forwarded to you, please sign up for our email list here. body:
| | | Hi, Adam Schiff here. It's my honor to serve as California's newest Senator and one of your DSCC Vice Chairs, fighting to flip the Senate blue.

**[In a moment, I'm going to tell you why we _must_ take back the Senate majority to stop Trump's agenda. But first, I need to ask: Will you donate $5 to the DSCC to make it happen?](https://go.dscc.org/go/21879?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250710-AdamSchiff-VCSchiffTrumpFRRF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250710-AdamSchiff-VCSchiffTrumpFRRF-Pros-sl1&akid=20718%2E9716520%2EAgnIR-)**

Let me make this as plain as possible: **Donald Trump isn't fit to lead.**

We see it in his actions every day. Pardoning January 6th defendants on his first day in office, trying to cut Medicaid to pay for massive tax cuts for the wealthiest Americans, attempting to end birthright citizenship, turning his back on our allies -- **if you invented a fictional politician hellbent on harming our country, they would look exactly like Donald Trump.**

But how have Senate Republicans responded to this shocking display? With shrugs, and -- more importantly -- _votes_ to let Donald Trump have his way with our government.

**And that is exactly why we _must_ take back the Senate: Republicans will _never_ stand up to Trump, so if _anyone_ is going to stop this madness and hold him accountable, the responsibility falls to Democrats like us.**

**[DSCC leadership, myself included, is counting on this team to raise $50,000 by midnight to keep our plan to flip the Senate on track._Nothing_ could be more important in these early days. So let me personally ask you -- will you rush $5 or more immediately to help fight for a Democratic majority? >>](https://go.dscc.org/go/21879?amount=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250710-AdamSchiff-VCSchiffTrumpFRRF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250710-AdamSchiff-VCSchiffTrumpFRRF-Pros-sl1&akid=20718%2E9716520%2EAgnIR-)**

name: The Democratic National Committee
subject: What needs your attention NOW →
body:
---
Derek, this is a longer email, but we have a big update for you. **If you can't stick around for every word, we'll cut straight to the chase: The DNC is ramping up our work against Trump and his allies, but we can’t do it without your support. Organizing and legal battles aren’t cheap, and we count on grassroots supporters like you to power our fight to elect Democrats who will fight for working families and communities nationwide.**[**If this work matters to you, please consider donating $3 to the DNC today >>**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-july-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250710_3925_fr&refcode2=3925_22930333_bvgJkv&akid=3925%2E22930333%2EbvgJkv) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._**[CONTRIBUTE $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-july-2025?amount=3&express_lane=true&refcode=em_20250710_3925_fr&refcode2=3925_22930333_bvgJkv&akid=3925%2E22930333%2EbvgJkv)** Here are the three things you need to know right now: **TRUMP’S BUDGET BILL HAS BEEN SIGNED INTO LAW** _This bill represents the largest transfer of wealth from the working class to the billionaire class in US history._ It will cut Medicaid and food assistance (like SNAP) for millions of Americans — while handing the ultrawealthy a giant tax break. Taking back Congress will be the key to stopping more harmful legislation like this — and that work has to start _now._ **WE’VE OFFICIALLY LAUNCHED ORGANIZING SUMMER** This summer, the DNC is investing in organizers in every state — red, blue, and purple — across the country to ensure that we’re reaching as many voters as we can. Taking back Congress and electing down-ballot Democrats who will fight against Trump and his allies is our highest priority right now, and we’re excited to get folks across the country involved. **WE’RE FIGHTING THE TRUMP ADMINISTRATION IN COURT TO PROTECT OUR ELECTIONS** Since Inauguration Day, the Trump administration has been trying to rewrite longstanding campaign finance laws, overturning decades of precedent. The DNC, along with the DSCC and DCCC, have filed a motion asking the Supreme Court to allow us to defend the federal election law that sets limits on coordinated party expenditures against the Republican Party’s challenge. This motion has been granted. **We’re fighting like hell against the Trump administration and its allies to protect our democracy and the American people. But organizing nationwide and taking the administration to court won’t be cheap.** [**Derek, we’re counting on your support to help us hold this administration and the Republican majority accountable. Please pitch in $3 to the DNC to help us elect Democrats up and down the ballot who will fight for the American people.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-july-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250710_3925_fr&refcode2=3925_22930333_bvgJkv&akid=3925%2E22930333%2EbvgJkv) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-july-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250710_3925_fr&refcode2=3925_22930333_bvgJkv&akid=3925%2E22930333%2EbvgJkv)**

name: Jon Ossoff
subject: New York Times: "People are going silent"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

Peter,
Before I ran for the U.S. Senate, I was an investigative journalist leading a team that exposed corruption and human rights abuses worldwide.
This report in _The New York Times_ reminded me of what I saw in many of those investigations:
[](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250710-1-f&t=1&akid=538%2E5691347%2EEKWCO_)
More and more, the atmosphere under Trump reminds me of countries where dissent is silenced by intimidation.
From the article:
_Fired federal workers who are worried about losing their homes ask not to be quoted by name. University presidents fearing that millions of dollars in federal funding could disappear are holding their fire. Chief executives alarmed by tariffs that could hurt their businesses are on mute. ..._
_“When you see important societal actors — be it university presidents, media outlets, C.E.O.s, mayors, governors — changing their behavior in order to avoid the wrath of the government, that’s a sign that we’ve crossed the line into some form of authoritarianism,” said Steven Levitsky, a professor of government at Harvard and the co-author of the influential 2018 book “How Democracies Die.”_
**This is unacceptable in the United States of America.**
Peter — As Trump dismantles checks on his power and purges the government to fill every office with loyalists, more and more Americans are too afraid of retaliation to speak up.
**This descent into an un-American America must be stopped. We must regain Congressional power.**
I’m the ONLY Democratic Senator up for re-election in a state Trump won.
That means I’m MAGA’s #1 target for defeat.
**Help me beat them again, just like we did in 2021.**
[**Please contribute to my campaign today.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250710-1-f&t=2&akid=538%2E5691347%2EEKWCO_)
Thank you for chipping in if you can.
\- Jon
**[DONATE](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-july25.vLE_dc&refcode=em250710-1-f&t=3&akid=538%2E5691347%2EEKWCO_)**

Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Thank you for supporting Jon Ossoff. Email is a critical way to keep up with Jon and his work in the US Senate, but you can click [here](https://act.electjon.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=538%2E5691347%2EEKWCO_) if you'd like to unsubscribe.

# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111238299/556597076/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2N2ZlZDMyNy1hNTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=XwiHLL2kuGVfVePCBye7C28ORdpD-DTBTNXZEeU0Rnk=)
Hi, it's Democratic Congressman and top Democrat on the House Oversight Committee, Robert Garcia.
As a proud Californian, trust me when I say that the road to retaking the House Majority for Democrats runs through California.
And since Republicans have just a narrow two-seat margin, it runs through three seats specifically:
→ California’s 40th District, currently held by Republican Young Kim
→ California’s 41st District, currently held by Republican Ken Calvert.
→ California’s 22nd District, currently held by Republican David Valadao.
_**And all three of them just voted to help pass Donald Trump’s horrifying bill into law.**_
So if David Valadao, Ken Calvert, and Young Kim are listening, I want them to hear it directly from me: **We’re going to make you pay for what you just did, we’re coming for your seats, and your days in Congress are numbered.**
**[But I need your help to get the job done. If you’re as fired up as I am to win back the House Majority and defeat these sellout Republicans, then chip in $10 or more right away. name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 7/10/2025
body:
“They called us hateful”: Colorado parents see their story reflected in Supreme Court ruling
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sbub5289d80)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s1k4747484c)**
| | Hi friends,
Listen to today's Mountain Minute podcast [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s0gca23fce4), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sdqd08e84ce) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s5re30e6b43)! Here is a summary of today's top stories:
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** State institutions are under fire this week. A scathing audit exposed major failures at the Colorado Bureau of Investigation, including over [1,000 mishandled forensic cases](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s2qa7682ffc). In Jefferson County, [33 sexual abuse and misconduct incidents](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sdn12ed1529) rocked the school district, while Rep. Ron Weinberg faces a [workplace probe](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s5n6eb802a1) after multiple sexual harassment accusations. Meanwhile, Colorado parents are feeling empowered by a recent Supreme Court ruling to[ challenge school policies](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s8h45d77d2c) they say silence their voices.
🇺🇸 **On the National Stage...** Federal leaders are cracking down on [Chinese threats to U.S. farmland](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s9scb21b62e) with new transparency rules, while lawmakers push to end NIH’s controversial [animal testing on dogs and cats](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s7o63a3cb77). The DOE warns that shutting down conventional power plants too quickly could [raise blackout risks](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sdv68868fa7). Meanwhile, President Trump is touting [over 2 million new jobs](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sepe0398e1c) for U.S.-born workers in 2025, crediting stricter border policies and a renewed focus on hiring Americans.
**🗣️ Commentary Spotlight...** Mike O’Donnell exposes how a [Biden-era “domestic terror” plan](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s0hffb9c621) opened the door to mass surveillance and censorship, with little oversight. John DiGirolamo warns of [AI's growing threat to children](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/scuf4caac1a), comparing deepfake tech and AI “boyfriends” to a real-life Terminator—calling on parents to wake up and protect their kids.
This is the Mountain Minute for Thursday, July 10, 2025.
– Team RMV
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[“They called us hateful”: Colorado parents see their story reflected in Supreme Court ruling](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s1m3c1d566b)** BY JEN SCHUMANN, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s3oa4cdfd21)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s1qde140bd9)
When the U.S. Supreme Court ruled that public schools must allow parents to opt their children out of LGBTQ+ instruction that conflicts with their religious beliefs, BJ and Brecken Jones sat quietly in their living room—stunned, relieved and vindicated.
“For years, we were treated like extremists for simply protecting our kids,” BJ said. “Now the Supreme Court says we were right all along.”
For this Boulder County family, Mahmoud v. Taylor wasn’t just a legal case—it was a mirror.
[Reading the Court’s opinion](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s3mdacf0af8), BJ and Brecken saw their own experience reflected almost line for line.
What they didn’t expect was that their quiet effort to opt out—rejected by school officials, scorned by activists and ultimately buried in district archives—would one day echo from the highest court in the country.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s8ia2d2be00)**
|
---
**[Jeffco Kids First report reveals 33 staff misconduct cases in Jefferson County Schools](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sbj281d4cb6)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s8ndf96de6f)
* A new report by grassroots group Jeffco Kids First has uncovered 33 cases of sexual abuse and misconduct by staff in Jefferson County Public Schools since 2022—many of which were never reported to the state.
* The report highlights disturbing failures in oversight, including staff quietly reassigned instead of removed, and a controversial “trusted adult” policy that enabled grooming under the guise of student support.
* Superintendent Tracy Dorland faces mounting criticism for a lack of transparency, and State Senator Barbara Kirkmeyer has called for her removal, citing persistent silence and inaction on student safety.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sal27a9da0b)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sdg7820fb21)
|
---
**[O’Donnell: The Strategic Plan that turned patriots into suspects remains unresolved](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s8hae235d6e)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s0t6f8b6117)
* A declassified 2021 Biden-era Strategic Implementation Plan enabled sweeping government coordination—across the FBI, DOJ, CIA, DHS, and others—to monitor and potentially target Americans expressing dissent, without Congressional approval or oversight.
* The plan funneled taxpayer dollars into initiatives like the Stanford Internet Observatory to censor posts questioning the 2020 election, COVID mandates, or Biden administration narratives—silencing citizens through shadow-banning and surveillance.
* Though President Trump has moved to reverse the plan, a 2025 GAO report found 49 out of 58 federal action items were already underway. O’Donnell warns the machinery of censorship and surveillance may still be running—just less visibly.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s7ndf8bb1df)**
|
---
** ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
**[DiGirolamo: The new Terminator is disguised as your teen’s ‘AI boyfriend’](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sco33b1c4ec)** **BY JOHN DIGIROLAMO****| COMMENTARY | JULY 9** _Make The Terminator fiction again. Perhaps it’s not a coincidence that the movie was released in 1984. The Hollywood hit focused on the sinister power of artificial intelligence in the form of a killer cyborg robot who looked human._
**[Boll: A day camp in Paonia shows how far the agenda to sexualize kids has gone](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s8g3d742150)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/sbk738502a9)****BY LAUREEN BOLL | COMMENTARY | JULY 8** _In the small, conservative town of Paonia, Colorado, with a population of just 1,500, a local organization is stirring debate with a new initiative aimed at children. The Learning Council, a tax-exempt nonprofit based in downtown Paonia, is hosting a “Sex Health Education Day Camp” for 10- to 13-year-olds on July 14–16._
__
**[Garbo: Let the purists prune—a thought exercise in Republican renewal](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s2kedbef619)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice._kCA/s1ua4b7a962)****BY C. J. GARBO | COMMENTARY | JULY 8** _The below op-ed is a thought exercise – a structured hypothetical meant to test assumptions, reveal contradictions, and sharpen our understanding by playing out ideas to their logical end. It is not a forecast or a policy proposal. It’s a lens designed to examine the mindset, strategy, and consequences of a particular ideological posture within the Republican Party_
__

**Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [tori@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\))) or send us a message on social media to chat!
|
---
|
---
**💧 DID YOU KNOW?** A **single drop of water** takes about 90 days to travel the full 2,340-mile length of the Mississippi River, according to the National Park Service. That's thanks to its slow-moving current, which ranges from 1.2 to 3 mph.
|
---
**CARTOON OF THE DAY**
|
---
**QUOTE OF THE DAY** "There is no art which one government sooner learns of another than that of draining money from the pockets of the people." – Adam Smith
|

# MEET THE CANDIDATES!

|
---
| |
---
| **LILY FRANKLIN FOR HD-41** Lily is running for delegate in Virginia’s 41st District because Southwest Virginia deserves a leader who will fight for us. Our communities are built on grit, hard work, and looking out for one another, but too many families are being left behind. She's spent nearly a decade working to ensure our region gets the resources it deserves, fighting for microgrants for farmers, housing protections, and clean drinking water laws. It’s time for a leader who will push for affordable housing, good jobs, strong schools, and better healthcare.

---

| |
---
| **JOY POWERS FOR HD-51** Joy Powers is running for delegate in District 51 to serve her community with real leadership that puts people first. Politics today is too often about division and party loyalty, but she believes in practical solutions that improve our daily lives—strong schools, good jobs, safer roads, support for first responders, farmers, and veterans, and protecting our rural way of life. Our community deserves a representative who works for us—not political points.

As a business and finance professional with 35 years at Carilion Clinic—in roles including Vice President of Finance, she knows how to balance budgets without cutting the care and support that our neighbors rely on. She believes in thoughtful leadership that respects our values, prioritizes fiscal responsibility, and ensures everyone has a fair shot at success.

**VA Works Assistance at The Roanoke City Main Library**
---
Do you need help with your job search? Looking for temporary income support or transition and training services? Visit the workforce experts at their satellite desks at the Main Library in downtown Roanoke. The Virginia Department of Workforce Development and Advancement is available at the Main Library Monday through Friday from 10 am to 4 pm.

**Washington Park Celebrates 102nd Anniversary**
---
On Saturday, we celebrated 102 years of Washington Park! Welcoming the community back in 1923, the Booker T. Washington Park, more commonly known now as Washington Park, was the first park for Black residents during segregation. Washington Park has remained a cherished place in our community, and it still enjoyed by Roanokers today.

**Natural Dyeing With Plants****\- July 10**
---
Join Carilion Clinic this afternoon from 6-7:30 pm at Morningside Urban Farm (917 Morgan Avenue SE) for the Natural Dyeing With Plants Workshop. Explore the colors of the garden through natural dyes and minerals! Learn the basics of using plants and flowers to dye fabrics and create beautiful prints. Artist and educator Alex Foster will teach the Japanese technique of “hapa-zome,” or “flower-pounding on cloth.”

**Tomato Fest at Grandin Village Farmers Market****\- July 12**
---
Summer sun means fat, juicy tomatoes galore! Join LEAP for their Grandin Village Farmers Market Tomato Fest! They're celebrating all things tomatoes—whether you like them best in salads or salsas, on a sandwich, or fried up for breakfast. The market vendors will be having fun with all kinds of tomatoes, big and small, round and wonky, beefsteak and heirloom, and they'll have some things to try. Come join in on the fun! The Grandin Village Farmers Market is open every Saturday from 8am – 12pm, April – November.

**Downtown Roanoke Architectural History Tour****\- July 12**
---
Take a walk through time at the Downtown Roanoke Architectural History Tour this Saturday at 10 am from the O. Winston Link Museum! Led by local historian Kelly Robertson, this 1.4-mile walking tour explores how Roanoke’s downtown evolved — and how its architecture tells the story of that transformation. Discover the rich variety of styles that define the city’s skyline, from historic landmarks to hidden gems, and gain new insight into the forces that shaped Roanoke’s unique urban landscape.

**Salem Fair****\- Now-July 13**
---
The Salem Fair is back! The Salem Fair is the largest fair in the state and has been recognized as one of the top 100 fairs and expositions in the country. The Salem Fair does not charge the public an admission fee for this event. Entry is free and you instead pay to ride rides, games, food and vendors. Parking is also free. The Salem Fair is located at the Salem Civic Center Complex.
