The market vendors will be having fun with all kinds of tomatoes, big and small, round and wonky, beefsteak and heirloom, and they'll have some things to try. Come join in on the fun! The Grandin Village Farmers Market is open every Saturday from 8am – 12pm, April – November. \[%7B%22mechanism%22%3A%22attachment%22%2C%22surface%22%3A%22newsfeed%22%7D\]%2C%22refnotiftype%22%3Anull%7D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiZTQ4ZjlmOS1hNjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=E8OOF5EiaRWR-jqpSutshfk1WNxmqeCX7XzBQQIcJjk=&emci=fe9e52ba-c75c-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=be48f9f9-a65d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=18273010 | | Downtown Roanoke Architectural History Tour\- July 12 Take a walk through time at the Downtown Roanoke Architectural History Tour this Saturday at 10 am from the O. Winston Link Museum! Led by local historian Kelly Robertson, this 1.4-mile walking tour explores how Roanoke’s downtown evolved — and how its architecture tells the story of that transformation. Discover the rich variety of styles that define the city’s skyline, from historic landmarks to hidden gems, and gain new insight into the forces that shaped Roanoke’s unique urban landscape. | Salem Fair\- Now-July 13 The Salem Fair is back! The Salem Fair is the largest fair in the state and has been recognized as one of the top 100 fairs and expositions in the country. The Salem Fair does not charge the public an admission fee for this event. Entry is free and you instead pay to ride rides, games, food and vendors. Parking is also free. The Salem Fair is located at the Salem Civic Center Complex. | | | | | | | | | | | | VSBA RECOGNIZES RCPS COMMUNITY BUILDERS Our Community Builders are doing great things! Thank you to the Virginia School Boards Association for recognizing our schools & students' achievements. The Community Builders program empowers students to achieve their dreams through fun, hands-on experiences outside the traditional classroom. | | | | Join the Roanoke City Democrats! RCDC's monthly business meeting is the third Thursday each month. Watch their Facebook for an upcoming announcement of the agenda. The meetings are available virtually for those who write to mailto:info@roanokedemocrats.com, requesting the link. | | Join Roanoke Young Dems! Meetings occur on the second Monday of every month in person and virtually. The goal of RYD is to help improve our community and to promote the names of Democratic candidates and policies. Contact: youngdemocratsroanoke@gmail.com to get involved! | | | | Legal Aid Society of Roanoke Valley The Legal Aid Society of Roanoke Valley provides a full range of civil legal services to low-income area residents, including issues involving housing, family, employment, consumer debt, and domestic violence. Free legal services are provided to qualified residents of the cities of Roanoke and Salem, along with the counties of Roanoke, Botetourt, Craig, Bedford, and Franklin. Email: info@lasrv.orgPhone: (540) 344-2080 | | Contact Your Federal Representatives In many ways, it feels like our federal government is under attack by the current administration. While our office is happy to assist however we can, it is still crucial that you engage our federal representatives. Find the contact information for the Virginia Congressional Delegation . | | | | KNOW YOUR RIGHTS WITH ICE There have been recent reports of ICE throughout the state. The ACLU has created a collection of resources in the case you or someone you know interacts with ICE agents. Read and share the resources found _. | | Thank you for your support! If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to reach out to me anytime. My very best, | | Del. Sam Rasoul | | | | | | ---|---|--- Paid for and Authorized By Friends of Sam Rasoul for Delegate Friends of Sam Rasoul for Delegate P.O. Box 13842 Roanoke, VA 24037 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Patty Murray
subject: Trump is coming for PBS and NPR next
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
body:
Next in Trump's crosshairs? Wait Wait...Don't Tell Me! Sesame Street. Mister Rogers' Neighborhood. / None
| | |
---
I'm disappointed. And I'm plain mad, Peter – but I am not surprised.

**Because as Donald Trump abuses his power to crack down on our First Amendment freedoms of speech and the press, he's on a warpath to take down PBS and NPR next.**
[If that's all you need to hear, then I need you to put it on record where you stand on federal funding for public broadcasting before my survey closes at midnight.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111241927/556600874/572439702?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk529-su-nd&refcode=jtk529-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYzQ4ZjlmOS1hNjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JecS1hPcipesUcCd9-9dT3JeA_DSGGuEVTWVrtshUKY=&emci=344f421e-ed5c-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=bc48f9f9-a65d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=13116610)

If this is the first you're hearing of this, let me catch you up:
**1.** Since Day One, Trump's second administration has shamelessly extorted media, educational, and legal institutions that report information he feels threatened by – _or simply dislikes_ – by illegally withholding federal funding.
**2.** Then, in May, he signed an executive order to slash public funding for PBS and NPR, accusing the outlets of taking **"millions from taxpayers to spread radical, woke propaganda."**
_(I suppose that "woke" now means lifesaving emergency alerts for rural communities with spotty cell service, impartial and non-sensational reporting, and beloved educational programs like "Sesame Street," and Ken Burns' documentaries.)_
**3. **So when he and _Project 2025-architect-turned-Cabinet-member_ Russ Vought proposed **zeroing out funding to all local public broadcasting stations through 2027,** House Republicans passed a bill to do just that.
So now the bill is coming to the Senate floor next week. I'll do everything I can to block this bill, but I need your help to drum up public pressure, Peter.
Fortunately, a recent survey found that **by a two-to-one margin, the American people overwhelmingly favor continuing federal funding for NPR and PBS. **But with the deck clearly stacked in Trump's favor, it's going to take _everyone_ who feels that way to make their voices heard.
**That's why I need to be sure that you and at least 4,999 more supporters are with me in the fight to save PBS and NPR –[so will you please take just two minutes to complete this survey before 11:59 p.m. tonight?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111241928/556600875/572439702?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk529-su-nd&refcode=jtk529-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYzQ4ZjlmOS1hNjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JecS1hPcipesUcCd9-9dT3JeA_DSGGuEVTWVrtshUKY=&emci=344f421e-ed5c-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=bc48f9f9-a65d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=13116610)**
Thanks for chiming in here, and for everything you do,

Patty
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/q9iNeaDCVUi-n6xIKf7j9g2?unsubscribedata=tamk22%2BhVkE4K03YTS3BOvto6mV%2BAOD3rYq3ZP9HcN4TyOJ20D7o%2BVeDnepimw%2FWzcIgzYV8Q%2ByDkBDjpv0el%2BUluNPEK6rUxxG%2FkhwMmzV9CcvAykfx7OrhLVSPDbBELlFMUN4yUGY4r33hvWrhoOSIbCAdM%2BPubEPgFKFfX0ZaQQKQdNTDBHpEEpguqRQqWgmXcS1qaa3wxEHRyf0bOtgmKtSr4s%2FttN9a%2Bj0QgjHpcLVJfaDxWc%2BDdzFLuW5DZxdsLbq7%2FeQtXIm%2FjGBPlXxV5Z6WW8RtdsX2O4N5GrVeoCzO%2FveyX2rW1eyUyOMmhh6xKcYLLIAUtllIfeYjm%2Fag%2FXBitwAqPbjbXJ1%2FhjL4MsTrtS4yMiACSYlEFGhWpfrQPWWrsRiAIkfFk4NW%2FmOu4biIrs%2Fhl5U3nd3Ga1VgFu%2BaUjoJFRhV0JpHcoFU) People for Patty Murray
PO BOX 3662
Seattle, WA 98124

name: America First Legal
subject: AFL Supporter Survey: We Need Your Opinion >>
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj205frqtdki217skbkxd93obmved3j6128gld82v3u022mzlx55p8nhgv1jqviyihyyzx4tbr7ozcb6k3opd&__stmp=sz6y65&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz6y65&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol00pzdq7uuqgczir680mstfjeqpzsbl28yu02vvuwer26vv5c8f00l7cb83u9hz1pr8bc1bv2qdux75khwe6914f5t6hlkmq0y1mla0gvp7t7in8rkx6r41nbtxwvwtq3e805tz8rtf&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz6y65&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz6y65&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz6y65&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz6y65&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=rc6qmltxol00pzdq7uuqgczir680mstfjeqpzsbl28yu02vvuwer26vv5c8f00l7cb83u9hz1pr8bc1bv2qdux75khwe6914f5t6hlkmq0y1mla0gvp7t7in8rkx6r41nbtxwvwtq3e805tz8rtf&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz6y65&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sz6y65&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

name: Eric Sorensen
subject: Sign On: Protect the Weather Service
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111245653/556606775/896231865?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmOGUyODllNC1hODVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7-ZHa_-ViJrI4EnaZsuKSjgsxmYDRtk_SKb8VrvkxUA=)
Over the weekend, devastating flash floods wreaked havoc across multiple states, killing at least 90 people in Texas alone. My heart is with the victims and their families. But the unfortunate truth is that this was completely avoidable, Peter. The regional weather service office’s warning coordination meteorologist, who organizes alerting the outside world about agency forecasts, took a Trump administration buyout in April. And the office’s Science Operations Officer, who implements new technology and data, also retired around the same time under DOGE pressure. **These are the real-time impacts of the far-right’s mindless war on climate science. It’s a costly reminder that cuts that might sound okay on a spreadsheet, are in fact highly-skilled, highly-trained specialists where the cost of catastrophe will far outweigh any savings.** As a meteorologist for more than two decades, I depended on these colleagues at the National Weather Service to provide accurate, life-saving weather reports for my neighbors. Our community stayed safe because of the information I gave them, that came from these organizations. **Now, we’re not just losing time that could have been spent innovating and leading in climate science, something we’ve proudly done for decades, but basic services – like alerting the public ahead of major storms – are being crippled.** This is a five-star alarm, and it’s important that we not look away while this is happening. As the only trained meteorologist in Congress, I am fighting back against any attempt to undermine the public servants at NOAA and the National Weather Service immediately. And I hope you’ll stand with me. [**That’s why I’m asking you once again to commit to continue mobilizing against the far-right’s attack on climate science. clean_body: | = Over the weekend, devastating flash floods wreaked havoc across multiple states, killing at least 90 people in Texas alone. My heart is with the victims and their families. But the unfortunate truth is that this was completely avoidable, Peter. The regional weather service office’s warning coordination meteorologist, who organizes alerting the outside world about agency forecasts, took a Trump administration buyout in April. And the office’s Science Operations Officer, who implements new technology and data, also retired around the same time under DOGE pressure. These are the real-time impacts of the far-right’s mindless war on climate science. It’s a costly reminder that cuts that might sound okay on a spreadsheet, are in fact highly-skilled, highly-trained specialists where the cost of catastrophe will far outweigh any savings. As a meteorologist for more than two decades, I depended on these colleagues at the National Weather Service to provide accurate, life-saving weather reports for my neighbors. Our community stayed safe because of the information I gave them, that came from these organizations. Now, we’re not just losing time that could have been spent innovating and leading in climate science, something we’ve proudly done for decades, but basic services – like alerting the public ahead of major storms – are being crippled. This is a five-star alarm, and it’s important that we not look away while this is happening. As the only trained meteorologist in Congress, I am fighting back against any attempt to undermine the public servants at NOAA and the National Weather Service immediately. And I hope you’ll stand with me. | -Eric | Eric Sorensen is a meteorologist born and raised in Rockford, IL representing IL-17 in Congress. He’s seen firsthand the importance of establishing trust with the community, and he’s determined to use his expertise as a climate communicator to fight for Illinoisans on environmental justice and more in Washington. To support Eric, = We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Thank you for supporting Eric Sorensen Eric Sorensen for Illinois P.O. Box 1172 Moline, IL 61265 United States

For too long, I’ve watched my state’s Senator, Mitch McConnell, and his GOP allies put special interests over our state, country, and Constitution. It’s unacceptable. As a veteran, I took an oath to defend our Constitution and the American people against any threat. I refuse to sit back and let Republicans continue to attack our access to food, healthcare, education, and other necessities we depend on. [As Kentucky’s US Senator, I will fight to protect our rights and freedoms, Peter. Will you help me build the grassroots power I’ll need to win by splitting a contribution between my campaign and Blue Amp Action today?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T2e4093fe-4061-46b7-8972-14f7a2f25bf7/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tdb0166ab-59f1-4d23-bb97-8e5420588398/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111245973/556607318/1895761198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMWU1ZTEwZi1hOTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sXjdu773cD0NMPJipmFcleBy8SWcr-n1yzqqcfBTgCA=&emci=b7ad50b6-a75d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=21e5e10f-a95d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=13185894) _**Largest VA Staff Cuts in History: 30,000 Staff to Be Gone by September**_ Earlier this year, the Trump administration set a goal to fire 83,000 employees from the VA. Now, the agency has announced that it’s on track to reduce its staff by 30,000 employees by September. **Derek, it’s clear what the end goal of all this is for Trump and his administration: To dismantle and weaken the VA so much that they have an excuse to keep up their privatization grift.** But I refuse to sit by while Trump and the GOP tries to gut the VA and use our veterans as pawns in their political games. I proudly wore the uniform and deployed several times in service to our country. So you better believe I’m doing everything I can to stop this. [**I’m leading the charge in the House against the privatization of the VA and defending my fellow veterans’ earned care. If you’re with me, please pitch in now to help me keep up the fight and stand for our veterans →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111245974/556607322/-898016266?refcode=email_071025_VA-cuts&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMWU1ZTEwZi1hOTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sXjdu773cD0NMPJipmFcleBy8SWcr-n1yzqqcfBTgCA=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | **** | **** | **** | **[Donate $5 →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111245975/556607324/-898016266?refcode=email_071025_VA-cuts&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&amount=5&express_lane=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMWU1ZTEwZi1hOTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sXjdu773cD0NMPJipmFcleBy8SWcr-n1yzqqcfBTgCA=)**

****
Here in Western Pennsylvania, we’ve already seen reports that Trump’s hiring freeze affected life-saving VA clinical trials and medical research happening right here— _veterans earned a heck of a lot more than that._
**That’s a betrayal.**
Where I’m from in Western PA, we know what it’s like to wear the uniform and have pride in our service. Here, patriotism isn’t just a word, it’s personal.
That’s why I won’t stand by while the Trump administration tries to privatize and sell the VA off to the highest bidder. I’m fighting back to defend the benefits and care that my fellow veterans earned—and I need you standing with me, Derek.

clean_body: Largest VA Staff Cuts in History: 30,000 Staff to Be Gone by September Earlier this year, the Trump administration set a goal to fire 83,000 employees from the VA. Now, the agency has announced that it’s on track to reduce its staff by 30,000 employees by September. Derek, it’s clear what the end goal of all this is for Trump and his administration: To dismantle and weaken the VA so much that they have an excuse to keep up their privatization grift. But I refuse to sit by while Trump and the GOP tries to gut the VA and use our veterans as pawns in their political games. I proudly wore the uniform and deployed several times in service to our country. body:
Tell us what’s at stake for you in Virginia this fall.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111248263/556610408/-793144256?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzIxZGY5Yi1hYTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=owzZx2NHmkQPzBx61-oxHHsXK6bqES5d_EdfkC7Iuu8=&emci=1421df9b-aa5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2c21df9b-aa5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15759664)
---
Derek, When we vote this November, we’re doing more than just checking off boxes on a ballot. We’ll be fighting for access to abortion, affordable healthcare, and a fairer future for all of our loved ones. [_**We want to know what you’ll be fighting for at the ballot box. Will you take our quick survey now?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111248264/556610409/-405611684?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzIxZGY5Yi1hYTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=owzZx2NHmkQPzBx61-oxHHsXK6bqES5d_EdfkC7Iuu8=&emci=1421df9b-aa5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2c21df9b-aa5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15759664) | **************[WHAT ARE YOU FIGHTING FOR?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111248267/556610410/-405611684?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzIxZGY5Yi1hYTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=owzZx2NHmkQPzBx61-oxHHsXK6bqES5d_EdfkC7Iuu8=&emci=1421df9b-aa5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2c21df9b-aa5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15759664)**************
---
I’ll go first:
**I’m fighting for my friends,** because no one should have to fear losing their rights because of who they are, where they come from, or what healthcare decisions they make.
**I’m fighting for the people I love,** because everyone deserves to live with dignity, access the care they need, and feel safe in their own community.
**And I’m fighting for the generations after us,** because they deserve clean air, safe communities, strong schools, and the right to vote in free and fair elections.
[_**Now, I want to hear from you. What are you fighting for this November?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111248270/556610411/-405611684?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDIzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyYzIxZGY5Yi1hYTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=owzZx2NHmkQPzBx61-oxHHsXK6bqES5d_EdfkC7Iuu8=&emci=1421df9b-aa5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2c21df9b-aa5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=15759664)

Your story matters, Derek. _Your fight matters._ And I’m grateful to be on your team.
Thank you,
**Kelsey Carolan**

clean_body: Tell us what’s at stake for you in Virginia this fall. | Derek, When we vote this November, we’re doing more than just checking off boxes on a ballot. We’ll be fighting for access to abortion, affordable healthcare, and a fairer future for all of our loved ones. | ********************** I’ll go first: I’m fighting for my friends, because no one should have to fear losing their rights because of who they are, where they come from, or what healthcare decisions they make. I’m fighting for the people I love, because everyone deserves to live with dignity, access the care they need, and feel safe in their own community. And I’m fighting for the generations after us, because they deserve clean air, safe communities, strong schools, and the right to vote in free and fair elections. ******************** The more we understand what’s driving our movement, the stronger and more united we’ll be when we show up at the ballot box this fall. * **CENTRAL TEXAS FLOODING NEWS**

**" Flood-related deaths rise to 96 in Kerr County as searches continue," **San Antonio Express-News'**Elizabeth Zavala** -- "As the massive search for flood victims entered its seventh day, local officials on Thursday said the death toll in Kerr County has risen to 96.

Officials have recovered 96 bodies in Kerr County as of 8 a.m. Thursday, with 60 of those adults and 36 children, said **Jonathan Lamb** , a spokesman with the Kerrville Police Department.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/34319e75-c508-4509-9616-62726af917f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Leitha and other state and local officials have said the search for victims will continue until every missing person is accounted for. Gov. **Greg Abbott** said Tuesday that 161 people could still be missing, and Lamb said on Thursday morning that number has not changed.

Among those still unaccounted for are five children and a counselor from Camp Mystic, a private summer camp for girls in Hunt along the Guadalupe River. Camp officials said this week that 27 campers and counselors died in the flooding.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/34319e75-c508-4509-9616-62726af917f2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Lamb said the focus for state and local officials remains on "rescue and reunification" operations. He said there are at least 100 response teams helping with rescue and recovery, and more than 2,100 people have taken part in the search.

The statewide total of flooding deaths has now risen to 119. Along with the 96 bodies recovered in Kerr County, eight have been recovered in neighboring Kendall County related to the same Guadalupe River flooding that claimed the lives in Kerr County. In a separate flooding incident, seven people died in Travis County, four in Williamson County and four in Burnet County, officials said.

Kerr County continues to clean up from the flooding, which inundated hundreds of homes and businesses. The floods caused $18 billion to $22 billion in damage throughout the Hill Country, according to an estimate by AccuWeather.

Lamb said a Federal Emergency Management Agency recovery center has been opened at Kerrville First Baptist Church, at 625 Washington St." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/b9d73bdd-9899-4e9d-84aa-c83d8405208a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

CENTRAL TEXAS FLOODING NEWS " Flood-related deaths rise to 96 in Kerr County as searches continue," San Antonio Express-News'Elizabeth Zavala -- "As the massive search for flood victims entered its seventh day, local officials on Thursday said the death toll in Kerr County has risen to 96. Officials have recovered 96 bodies in Kerr County as of 8 a.m. Thursday, with 60 of those adults and 36 children, said Jonathan Lamb , a spokesman with the Kerrville Police Department. Leitha and other state and local officials have said the search for victims will continue until every missing person is accounted for. Gov. Greg Abbott said Tuesday that 161 people could still be missing, and Lamb said on Thursday morning that number has not changed. Among those still unaccounted for are five children and a counselor from Camp Mystic, a private summer camp for girls in Hunt along the Guadalupe River. Camp officials said this week that 27 campers and counselors died in the flooding. Lamb said the focus for state and local officials remains on "rescue and reunification" operations. He said there are at least 100 response teams helping with rescue and recovery, and more than 2,100 people have taken part in the search. The statewide total of flooding deaths has now risen to 119. Along with the 96 bodies recovered in Kerr County, eight have been recovered in neighboring Kendall County related to the same Guadalupe River flooding that claimed the lives in Kerr County. In a separate flooding incident, seven people died in Travis County, four in Williamson County and four in Burnet County, officials said. Kerr County continues to clean up from the flooding, which inundated hundreds of homes and businesses. The floods caused $18 billion to $22 billion in damage throughout the Hill Country, according to an estimate by AccuWeather. Lamb said a Federal Emergency Management Agency recovery center has been opened at Kerrville First Baptist Church, at 625 Washington St." ($) FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Leitha and other state and local officials have said the search for victims will continue until every missing person is accounted for. Gov. **Greg Abbott** said Tuesday that 161 people could still be missing, and Lamb said on Thursday morning that number has not changed.

Among those still unaccounted for are five children and a counselor from Camp Mystic, a private summer camp for girls in Hunt along the Guadalupe River. Camp officials said this week that 27 campers and counselors died in the flooding.

Lamb said the focus for state and local officials remains on “rescue and reunification” operations. He said there are at least 100 response teams helping with rescue and recovery, and more than 2,100 people have taken part in the search.

The statewide total of flooding deaths has now risen to 119. Along with the 96 bodies recovered in Kerr County, eight have been recovered in neighboring Kendall County related to the same Guadalupe River flooding that claimed the lives in Kerr County. In a separate flooding incident, seven people died in Travis County, four in Williamson County and four in Burnet County, officials said.

Kerr County continues to clean up from the flooding, which inundated hundreds of homes and businesses. The floods caused $18 billion to $22 billion in damage throughout the Hill Country, according to an estimate by AccuWeather.

Lamb said a Federal Emergency Management Agency recovery center has been opened at Kerrville First Baptist Church, at 625 Washington St." [SAEN](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eLA4v3xJ7EFyLSWgpDk4sY80J3xM3AWUsD9Hj-8dLLT4Q3V0h3gRFrx7dDruWb3J3Yf1gnpx28sO45cFBAR-nzXIf-P4drYqBq-oq_ALU_b_0wE2o1LafDdgHLJat8KzNTdk_Kmh8Kge_2KO1eXo7LG1HZpUzTIngxGMe4CcWSknN0bZMLOqh-R69ytGvPC71fWEJSHi1ALDKP0wQrTqDlLZgY07BEktYbA1V1lJTivGScqLtvoJyvbsoP6KkRzLiwGjini67wuhn2wNpsF_Kccj3qURYoljuSGjGaZmBuMjZgJ5_LIRMNx-SOhxTGfoHoWXoSzdQ-t57wp6xnzI9trWZpmWo2Pt6z1f85EfdjJrMCAxH9R19cbrhKmPhyljDVsWaMXdN_jUBr7kNEsUe8qe64F_QT2KpqZ3f_68YI8HdgOBSSXDLKf6noLe-4yw_JGFBnwVkoWSfWq-HQCEZrTglQTOFXEbSc18g3sNLM3pvr0EfaQpf4BmStoMk4_8Zg/4i2/WJkZTp8_SFaQ6XBMkEUOSg/h12/h001.Eg6-X4VxCAv_7RDtLLUewz22pvWe6xcaXFjrthfr-eY) ($)

Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr | CENTRAL TEXAS FLOODING NEWS “Flood-related deaths rise to 96 in Kerr County as searches continue,” San Antonio Express-News'Elizabeth Zavala — “As the massive search for flood victims entered its seventh day, local officials on Thursday said the death toll in Kerr County has risen to 96. Officials have recovered 96 bodies in Kerr County as of 8 a.m. Thursday, with 60 of those adults and 36 children, said Jonathan Lamb , a spokesman with the Kerrville Police Department. » Leitha and other state and local officials have said the search for victims will continue until every missing person is accounted for. Gov. Greg Abbott said Tuesday that 161 people could still be missing, and Lamb said on Thursday morning that number has not changed. Among those still unaccounted for are five children and a counselor from Camp Mystic, a private summer camp for girls in Hunt along the Guadalupe River. Camp officials said this week that 27 campers and counselors died in the flooding. » Lamb said the focus for state and local officials remains on “rescue and reunification” operations. He said there are at least 100 response teams helping with rescue and recovery, and more than 2,100 people have taken part in the search. The statewide total of flooding deaths has now risen to 119. Along with the 96 bodies recovered in Kerr County, eight have been recovered in neighboring Kendall County related to the same Guadalupe River flooding that claimed the lives in Kerr County. In a separate flooding incident, seven people died in Travis County, four in Williamson County and four in Burnet County, officials said. Kerr County continues to clean up from the flooding, which inundated hundreds of homes and businesses. The floods caused $18 billion to $22 billion in damage throughout the Hill Country, according to an estimate by AccuWeather. Lamb said a Federal Emergency Management Agency recovery center has been opened at Kerrville First Baptist Church, at 625 Washington St." ($) | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. * **CENTRAL TEXAS FLOODING NEWS**

**" Flood-related deaths rise to 96 in Kerr County as searches continue," **San Antonio Express-News'**Elizabeth Zavala** -- "As the massive search for flood victims entered its seventh day, local officials on Thursday said the death toll in Kerr County has risen to 96.

Officials have recovered 96 bodies in Kerr County as of 8 a.m. Thursday, with 60 of those adults and 36 children, said **Jonathan Lamb** , a spokesman with the Kerrville Police Department.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/b4cf6219-9772-4ceb-afe2-b158ec3bc320?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Leitha and other state and local officials have said the search for victims will continue until every missing person is accounted for. Gov. **Greg Abbott** said Tuesday that 161 people could still be missing, and Lamb said on Thursday morning that number has not changed.

Among those still unaccounted for are five children and a counselor from Camp Mystic, a private summer camp for girls in Hunt along the Guadalupe River. Camp officials said this week that 27 campers and counselors died in the flooding.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/b4cf6219-9772-4ceb-afe2-b158ec3bc320?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Lamb said the focus for state and local officials remains on "rescue and reunification" operations. He said there are at least 100 response teams helping with rescue and recovery, and more than 2,100 people have taken part in the search.

The statewide total of flooding deaths has now risen to 119. Along with the 96 bodies recovered in Kerr County, eight have been recovered in neighboring Kendall County related to the same Guadalupe River flooding that claimed the lives in Kerr County. In a separate flooding incident, seven people died in Travis County, four in Williamson County and four in Burnet County, officials said.

Kerr County continues to clean up from the flooding, which inundated hundreds of homes and businesses. The floods caused $18 billion to $22 billion in damage throughout the Hill Country, according to an estimate by AccuWeather.

Lamb said a Federal Emergency Management Agency recovery center has been opened at Kerrville First Baptist Church, at 625 Washington St." [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/c3cab920-ba95-43e8-aec6-a6cbb16c2326?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

subject: The Courage to Stay In Conversation
body:
A people divided can't protect each other. Here's how we begin to rebuild our trust, truth, and togetherness.

| |
---|---|---

# [The Courage to Stay In Conversation](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=168005099&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDA1MDk5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTIxNjUzMTUsImV4cCI6MTc1NDc1NzMxNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TcXqpQsnjJN7Nvqmd6C_AKbBoyq4sSnKnVp4VzENdYo)
### A people divided can't protect each other. Here's how we begin to rebuild our trust, truth, and togetherness.
| | [Melissa Chaudhry](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---
| Jul 10
---
| [](https://substack.com/@melissa4congress)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=168005099&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDA1MDk5LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MjE2NTMxNSwiZXhwIjoxNzU0NzU3MzE1LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMzI2MDExMSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.iSritX9pIW6Gxl7fh-A6l-uAVHvdi3PYCZd7-WGSnvA&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=168005099&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDA1MDk5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTIxNjUzMTUsImV4cCI6MTc1NDc1NzMxNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TcXqpQsnjJN7Nvqmd6C_AKbBoyq4sSnKnVp4VzENdYo&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=3260111&post_id=168005099&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY4MDA1MDk5LCJpYXQiOjE3NTIxNjUzMTUsImV4cCI6MTc1NDc1NzMxNSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTMyNjAxMTEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.TcXqpQsnjJN7Nvqmd6C_AKbBoyq4sSnKnVp4VzENdYo)
---
| | [](https://substack..yPOJYMQM9K9UJtJx_xuJwZHRE-2rDAPdTFMV_1kPtzk?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/melissa4congress/p/the-courage-to-stay-in-conversation?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

In the last post, I said something bold:
> _If we can 't talk to each other, we can't work together -- and if we can't work together, we won't survive what's coming._
That wasn't a metaphor. It's not just a nice idea about being more civil. I'm talking about a skillset -- a life-or-death one.
We're living in a time of rising authoritarianism, disinformation, and social fragmentation. But the real danger isn't just the bad actors -- it's that the rest of us don't know how to stay in hard conversations long enough to form _real alliances_ with one another.
We don't know how to speak across pain, fear, difference, and mistrust. And if we don't change that -- fast -- we'll remain too splintered to defend each other.
So today, I want to introduce the _methodology_ behind this project -- not just for political communication, but for reclaiming our capacity to be **a people** again.
* * *
## 🧭 Five Anchors for Transformative Conversation
These are the inner muscles we need to grow -- as individuals, and as a collective:
### 1\. **Nuance**
Nuance means refusing to collapse complexity into false binaries.
It's the art of saying: _both things can be true._
It's not compromise for the sake of comfort -- it's **precision** for the sake of truth.
Nuance lets us:
* understand how people get pulled into harmful ideologies without excusing them
* hold grief and accountability side by side
* break out of loyalty to "team" in favor of loyalty to _life_

🧠 _Nuance is what happens when we slow down enough to think -- and care -- at the same time._
* * *
### 2\. **Empathy**
Empathy is not agreement. It's the capacity to feel with.
It starts with the magic of **validation** -- that moment when someone feels seen, heard, and understood, even if you don't share their view.
It dissolves tension like water softens clay.
And suddenly, where there was defensiveness, there's room to breathe.
When we validate someone's inner world -- "I see this is really painful for you," or "I hear that you're scared" -- we open a doorway. Often it's the only one that will work.
Empathy asks: _What fear is driving this? What need is unmet?_
🪶 _A person who feels heard is a person who can grow._
* * *
### 3\. **Memory**
Memory is not just history. It's emotional context.
It's your grandmother's warning.
It's the ancestral story in your blood.
It's the lived experience behind a political view.
It's the muscle memory of betrayal, or hope, or grief.
We dishonor people when we ask them to "be rational" without holding space for the histories -- personal, familial, cultural -- that shaped them.
Memory is also what helps us resist repetition. We remember so we don't recreate the very things we thought we'd escaped.
📜 _Without memory, we lose both accountability and continuity. We forget who we are._
* * *
### 4\. **Humanity**
This one is deeply personal to me.
When I was younger, an older brother figure once told me:
> "When you see everyone as a child, you'll know how to treat them."
Not in a patronizing way. But with the eyes of compassion.
If someone were five, or eight, or ten -- throwing a tantrum, shutting down, lashing out -- we'd ask _what happened to them?_
We'd offer guidance. Boundaries. Gentleness. Safety.
That's humanity: the active commitment to **see the child** in others -- even when their adult armor is covered in spikes.
🙇🏽♀️ _Humanizing someone else often begins with remembering our own fragility._
* * *
### 5\. **Courage**
Courage is the choice to stay in the room.
To stay present when you feel triggered.
To breathe through the first wave of fear or rage.
To listen long enough to locate the _human_ underneath the argument.
There's a passage from _Prayers of the Cosmos_ , a translation of Jesus' Aramaic teachings, that I think of often:
> _" Love your enemy" in Aramaic means something more like, "Find ways to make your deep rhythm harmonize with theirs, when they are jagged against you. This kind of love creates. It doesn't just emote."_
That's courage. Not passivity, but **transformational tension-holding**.
It's the courage to refuse cynicism. To see a possible future -- and speak from there.
🗣️ _Moral courage is the birthplace of real conversation. Without it, we stay silent -- or cruel._
* * *
## 🧘🏽 A Practice for Listening
To ease our way in these kinds of conversations, we need to **listen differently**. Here's how:
1. **Ground yourself** :
Breathe. Feel your feet. Remind yourself: _I don 't have to fix this. I just have to stay present._
2. **Notice your triggers** :
Is your chest tight? Your jaw? What are you defending? Just name it silently.
3. **Look for the child** :
Imagine this person at five years old. What wound or need might they be carrying?
4. **Validate before challenging** :

> "I hear that you're scared..."
> "It sounds like you really care about safety..."
> Say _their_ values before you speak yours.
1. **Speak from your rooted place** :
Not from reactivity. Not to win. But from the place that says: _I 'm here. I still see you. And I still believe in dignity - yours, mine, and everyone's._

* * *
## 🧰 The Tools We'll Use
The good news? We don't have to invent all this from scratch. We're going to draw from some of the most powerful communication tools out there -- adapted for this moment of political and social rupture.
🔹 **Nonviolent Communication (NVC):** speaking from needs and values, not blame or judgment
🔹 **Narrative reclamation:** telling deeper stories that reflect the whole truth -- and giving people a role to play in those stories
🔹 **Appeal to shared principles:** finding common ground not in opinions, but in what we _value_
🔹 **Future-casting:** helping people imagine a future worth choosing -- and the role they could play in getting there
🔹 **Active humanizing:** the subtle art of describing others in ways that reawaken dignity
* * *
## 🧪 This Is Not Just Theory
Each post here will be a **lab** -- a place where we learn, test, practice, and reflect.
Sometimes we'll take a hot-button issue (immigration, the Middle East, AI, democracy) and walk through how to talk about it _without losing our souls_.
Sometimes we'll look at how to invite others into our values without shame.
Sometimes we'll unpack our own reactions -- and learn to hold our center.
Each post will include an **invitation to practice** -- because this is not about being right. It's about becoming whole. Together.
* * *
## 🫂 An Invitation
In a time like this, some people retreat into fear. Others lash out.
But there's another path -- the quiet, powerful path of learning how to speak again. How to listen again.
If you're here, you're probably one of the brave ones. You feel the stakes.
You know we can't build a future unless we learn how to become _we the people_.
So I invite you to:
* **subscribe** and **share** , if you haven't
* **comment** with your thoughts or questions
* **try the practice** below:

Let's make this real.
* * *
## 🏁 Practice Prompt
This week, notice a moment -- online or in person -- when you see someone say something you strongly disagree with.
Ask yourself:
1. _What might this person be afraid of?_
2. _What value might they be trying to protect?_
3. _How could I speak to that value -- not just the opinion?_

Try it, if you feel safe. Even in your journal. Even in your heart.
This is how we begin.
* * *
**PS:** What else would you add to this list of skills? What has helped _you_ stay in conversation with people you disagree with? Share in the comments -- I'll be reading them all.

Winning in August would diminish the GOP’s power in Georgia – and it’d be a disastrous upset for Trump and his authoritarian buddies.
Anything is possible when we come together and support candidates fighting for the causes that voters care about most. It’ll be tough, but **this seat is critical to building Democratic power in the legislature, so we can’t give up.**
**Letting far-right power go unchecked fuels hate and rolls back our rights. But together we can help support state Dems who are committed to building a better, safer, and more inclusive future.**
[Here’s the bottom line: Winning elections like this requires grassroots support. clean_body: Georgia Democrats are facing a key special election for a seat in the state Senate, and the stakes are sky high. We’re fighting to flip as many seats as possible in this ultra-competitive chamber. | | | | | ---|--- On August 26th, Georgia Democrats are facing a key special election for a seat in the state Senate, and the stakes are sky high. Debra Shigley is the lone Democrat in a race against Republicans in a GOP-held district. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | | | | | Winning in August would diminish the GOP’s power in Georgia – and it’d be a disastrous upset for Trump and his authoritarian buddies. Anything is possible when we come together and support candidates fighting for the causes that voters care about most. It’ll be tough, but this seat is critical to building Democratic power in the legislature, so we can’t give up. Letting far-right power go unchecked fuels hate and rolls back our rights. But together we can help support state Dems who are committed to building a better, safer, and more inclusive future. Are you ready to fight back? # [Pete Hegseth is jeopardizing national security — remove him NOW](https://default.salsalabs.org/T64c2e568-6677-44f2-9731-c0f46ffa6ad3/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

---
| | | In a move that shocked even fellow Republicans, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth [unilaterally paused weapons shipments to Ukraine](https://default.salsalabs.org/T513c7b25-c694-4244-815c-3100f21611fb/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9), including desperately needed Patriot missiles, crippling that nation's defense — all without informing President Trump or Congress. That alone would be bad enough. But it gets worse. Hegseth also reportedly shared sensitive U.S. war plans via unsecured messaging apps, triggering an internal investigation by the Pentagon's Inspector General, and leading to bipartisan agreement that Hegseth is a jackass. He's not just reckless — he's dangerous. And he's still running the Pentagon. Congressional Democrats and even some Republicans are calling for new leadership for Defense. Now we need grassroots pressure to back them up. [**Add your name now: Demand Congress remove Pete Hegseth before he causes even more damage.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Td6805362-3c99-456c-b2a1-c6a242b605c1/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

name: JanePAC.com
subject: 5 hidden environmental disasters you missed in Trump’s spending bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee
body:
Don't let them get away with this.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111253882/556615770/1328794144?refcode=ea.f75aab37-ae5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ms=ea.f75aab37-ae5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzk2NzgyIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImY3NWFhYjM3LWFlNWQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=75dQOKYpa84hukIH4kZO7RwrxoYoXnW1PWFdADsOv4A=&emci=797031c2-fc5c-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=f75aab37-ae5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=4823105)
---
Heads up: By now, you probably know that Trump's devastating spending bill will slash clean energy incentives and hand out massive giveaways to the fossil fuel industry. What you might not know is that the 887-page monstrosity he just signed into law is stuffed with so many environmental attacks, we could be here all day listing them all. **We want to shine a light on 5 egregious anti-environmental provisions you may have missed:** | **☠️ 1. Methane polluters just got a 10-year free pass.** The new law postpones the methane leak fee for oil and gas operations by 10 years and rescinds funding for other programs designed to reduce methane leaks. **🚷 2. The National Park Service lost $267 million in staff funding.** During a staffing crisis and record-breaking visitation, Trump ripped vital funding from the very people who protect these historic treasures and keep people safe. **🐾 3. Endangered species recovery plans were rescinded.** The law guts the funding designed to restore endangered and threatened species. Wildlife species that were already on the brink of extinction just lost critical lifelines. **🌩️ 4. They slashed funding for weather forecasting.** The law eliminates $150 million for improving storm prediction and climate monitoring — money that would’ve gone to NOAA scientists and life-saving early warning systems. **⛏️ 5. Four million acres of federal lands just got opened up for coal mining.** And to top it off, Trump added a brand-new tax break for steelmaking coal producers — just to sweeten the deal for Big Coal — all while cutting clean energy manufacturing credits and letting wind and solar projects wither.
---
These cuts will destroy decades of climate progress and impact millions of Americans, especially folks in low-income and frontline communities who are already bearing the brunt of the climate crisis. **Our kids and grandkids will inherit a more polluted, more dangerous world so fossil fuel execs can get richer.**
Trump and every Republican who voted for this bill are counting on us to throw our hands up and walk away. That's not happening. We're staying united and committed to electing climate champions who aren’t afraid to take on the fossil fuel industry and will deliver real solutions for our communities.

clean_body: Don't let them get away with this. | Heads up: By now, you probably know that Trump's devastating spending bill will slash clean energy incentives and hand out massive giveaways to the fossil fuel industry. What you might not know is that the 887-page monstrosity he just signed into law is stuffed with so many environmental attacks, we could be here all day listing them all. We want to shine a light on 5 egregious anti-environmental provisions you may have missed: | ☠️ 1. Methane polluters just got a 10-year free pass. The new law postpones the methane leak fee for oil and gas operations by 10 years and rescinds funding for other programs designed to reduce methane leaks. 🚷 2. The National Park Service lost $267 million in staff funding. During a staffing crisis and record-breaking visitation, Trump ripped vital funding from the very people who protect these historic treasures and keep people safe. 🐾 3. Endangered species recovery plans were rescinded. The law guts the funding designed to restore endangered and threatened species. Wildlife species that were already on the brink of extinction just lost critical lifelines. 🌩️ 4. They slashed funding for weather forecasting. The law eliminates $150 million for improving storm prediction and climate monitoring — money that would’ve gone to NOAA scientists and life-saving early warning systems. ⛏️ 5. Four million acres of federal lands just got opened up for coal mining. And to top it off, Trump added a brand-new tax break for steelmaking coal producers — just to sweeten the deal for Big Coal — all while cutting clean energy manufacturing credits and letting wind and solar projects wither. These cuts will destroy decades of climate progress and impact millions of Americans, especially folks in low-income and frontline communities who are already bearing the brunt of the climate crisis. Our kids and grandkids will inherit a more polluted, more dangerous world so fossil fuel execs can get richer. Trump and every Republican who voted for this bill are counting on us to throw our hands up and walk away. That's not happening. We're staying united and committed to electing climate champions who aren’t afraid to take on the fossil fuel industry and will deliver real solutions for our communities. Onward, – Team JanePAC | = | = = Follow along: | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|--- Paid for by Jane Fonda Climate PAC, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee. Jane Fonda Climate PAC 600 Pennsylvania Ave SE #15180 Washington, DC 20003 United States

body:
I’m counting on YOU to add your name to save me.

I’m counting on YOU to add your name to save me.

---
My God, Friend...
---
_Has it really come to this?_
---
I always knew the price I could one day pay for standing by the truth and the side of my good friend, our 45th and 47th President.
---
But what the radical Democrats and the Deep State are trying to do to those of us who’ve _REFUSED_ to surrender to tyranny will destroy our once free Republic forever.
---
**Today, all due to bogus state-level charges schemed up by a Democrat Attorney General in Arizona, I'm staring down the possibility of spending the rest of my years in PRISON!**
---
My crime? Helping to defend Donald J. Trump from one of the most vile witch hunts in American history.
---
_And on top of it all, because the Democrats concocted these sham criminal charges at the _state level_ , President Trump won’t even be able to issue a federal pardon to save me. _
---
But rest assured, while things may look bleak, I will **NEVER,_EVER_ SURRENDER**!
---
My whole life, I’ve always been known as a fighter. And at 80 years old, I’m _still_ fighting back against these Deep State goons!
---
**And while the President cannot save me from the Democrats’ vindictive scheme, there is _ONE_ thing that still can... **
---
**_...The collective voice of MILLIONS of patriots like YOU._**
---
That’s why today, I’m asking you to ADD YOUR NAME to the [**“Rudy Giuliani Freedom Petition: DROP ALL CHARGES!”**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zjhmj1fflchf1m7tb8ek5tgi5vr7kkvmiibokim0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2chge8oj2cth2bs)

__
---

Friend, the leftist thugs who had me unjustly booked in one of the most violent jails in America filed my mugshot under **booking # 2313757**.
---
_So, how fitting would it be to collect**2,313,757** petition signatures from true America patriots like you demanding they DROP ALL CHARGES? _
---
[**Please help me reach this life-saving (_truly!_) goal today by immediately adding your name to the “Rudy Giuliani Freedom Petition: DROP ALL CHARGES!”**](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2znvvbjdcbfqhf8n60u1833fjkoa5mosg4ib7qji0_rp22sh2s8i6epb2chge8oj2cth2bs)

clean_body: This isn’t policy. It’s cruelty. Folks, Last week, Republicans passed a budget bill that will close rural hospitals. Shut down nursing homes. Kick tens of millions of Americans off their health insurance, including seniors, working families, and vulnerable children. All to pay for another massive tax break for the ultra-wealthy. Martin Heinrich is fighting tooth and nail to hold the GOP accountable for their attack on our health care. But here’s the truth: if we’re going to hold Republicans accountable, we need to build serious grassroots power, and we’ve got to do it right now. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Martin believes health care is a right, not just a luxury for the wealthy. But Republicans in Congress have once again proven they’ll sacrifice the well-being of millions just to reward billionaire donors and corporate insiders. We can’t let them get away with it. Let’s make sure every single one of them feels the heat and knows we’re not backing down. Thanks for being part of this fight, Team Heinrich | Paid for by Martin Heinrich for Senate Senator Martin Heinrich is committed to helping New Mexico become a leader in defense, tech, and energy with opportunities in every corner. Fellow Conservative,

I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to.

Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: _**Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss.**_ While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos.

Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away.

I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— _**just weeks after 9/11.**_

_**I didn’t wait. I went.**_

And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map.

I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter.

Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign.
_**

[**HELP ME PROTECT THE AMERICA WE LOVE

This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. **But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for.**

And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for.

We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest.

**If you believe in what I’m fighting for,[even $5 goes a long way.](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=701a45167f984d3e8fe9257d2b213602&_e=V4ectjnJerLiUQ_BlPmLSWU064poEZSsS_LE9N_MsBxmA7GVoRTF63KqDvHcd-tnHN4xtL_rzXG0vXSWLOCviALgSSpB2z90C0_X4oAk0CCszuNOs1erH4h4rEE9zLPh-ULurfmRphpUXh3YCdJWajuOXWpvEOXglPBufaBZ_-H96usmM_sWV-LhXei_Fom3CCxOSruM7zBWGeZApVU0mr2m6OHXOVYOQz7SeumUpjfLt3TfCd6VozwV7og7gucHNSWcSoZfOdUjnU2iYYdGYN1mRrRWYgIjD-PKoEk6g9wqBEgg21BjBIMBguGp-ZS8ZVo2b0npRejay1zbdamoTVSPOLEai8T0hf48il2fS6evVT0-PqNGLTLrCg0L_nCFT7oj4ZZ819-AfT4jSBKYkrwvHRZslFDbForg1L8dWcQOA1pbhnw1ETHgiiYmqiCsin7ozwPFnSiuyLxyfFEVkJMIV8Bv7CjhU1QCnCx-BmWgX5Q23chT5f5z6XFKxpgqZsoUdNwfw1yNZi9Wn0H4Ww1nlKoSR3eayM-_h-5HlIle1MRs-89q7UUdV49ESYSvt0wAZbRQ-2IMgrc_sNSF_yZIjHM55Ga4HcbN4aZ0J2oe7SzPT3mhXeJsW3Okndn_H7do3FV8rLgp8xVMgMWozp7P0C0i4MeUqelMJK5JaxvfPoFv4hTGwS3mgno9whbW5lyHmwEvL2fkvxBWwf2A3ul3AOpmqqvyyBKdEowL-JVY_moKaD_ZGX_RS-ImqSePLyMwt19byscAdGXb5AuND4P3fLPQ9HtsJmvHaUKrbmk%3D)**

If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now.

Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together.

**You’ve done your part for this country.[Let me do mine—will you donate $15 to help?](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=701a45167f984d3e8fe9257d2b213602&_e=V4ectjnJerLiUQ_BlPmLSWU064poEZSsS_LE9N_MsBxmA7GVoRTF63KqDvHcd-tnHN4xtL_rzXG0vXSWLOCviALgSSpB2z90C0_X4oAk0CCszuNOs1erH4h4rEE9zLPh-ULurfmRphpUXh3YCdJWajuOXWpvEOXglPBufaBZ_-H96usmM_sWV-LhXei_Fom3CCxOSruM7zBWGeZApVU0mr2m6OHXOVYOQz7SeumUpjfLt3TfCd6VozwV7og7gucHNSWcSoZfOdUjnU2iYYdGYN1mRrRWYgIjD-PKoEk6g9zKA-v2hch4pfbJVqeeoiqvFCEV0SBlKDX2ksjjthWmnydhYlz2L2HlNdD0Urw-uCrEOG0Cu7xGCL81cOMSqCmiBlwByZBqYMkDdcfzS3QuMUIRPX2BrvwoZ8ZTcnV0BjIM6XN7qiZaTxqwD0cikhn9PQCaVXR-UYWJaoPSt5mfbW97KHz6XPQ7gllRyRRJ14_JjUcXLWCn9TxDstg0dJ2w5NXSs7e7cS-Kvgckv4pnPNCaysmOD4ovty8jiveDQywyg3U7vBvI_7M2zN6eIR50gYisBS7FY9hqP1vCGxbU6D0szKc_k6yWwuthXks6mnE9plhocvOjwwXv0uk47wIh4oK1op_KkUvep7lFkrBy2zs2-jw060WPwFG92V3Xt6djHGJzhG7qYWopK9Q5YBjuSKwsdyrLiguCUgIF3vHiIGpRRvKiDw-atK0v93aEz9Q0kudliLj2HxndUGJ2PmnNSa_JEEt72d7UDP03hxurqIUizb6oY_3Ue_rewqrShOE%3D)**

clean_body: I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for. | Fellow Conservative, I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to. Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss. While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos. Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away. I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— just weeks after 9/11. I didn’t wait. I went. And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map. I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter. Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign. Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win. This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for. And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for. We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest. If you believe in what I’m fighting for, If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now. Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together. You’ve done your part for this country. In liberty Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. body:
Emily recorded a quick message for you on Friday, right after the vote on the Big Bad Bill — the budget that slashes Medicaid and food assistance for our neighbors and gives the 1% a huge tax break.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111257221/556626569/2145945666?refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.07.10_BigBadBill2_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmQ5ZTQ4ZC1iNDVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9KEYG258os9vKZASG5W_vd2GnCYCmdxpgLK1OVd2BWM=&emci=e7793766-0f5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2fd9e48d-b45d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) | Peter — Emily recorded a quick message for you on Friday, right after the vote on the Big Bad Bill — the budget that slashes Medicaid and food assistance for our neighbors and gives the 1% a huge tax break. [_**You can watch her video on Instagram here**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111257222/556626570/-757686477?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.07.10_BigBadBill2_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmQ5ZTQ4ZC1iNDVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9KEYG258os9vKZASG5W_vd2GnCYCmdxpgLK1OVd2BWM=&emci=e7793766-0f5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2fd9e48d-b45d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) and read her note below. Together, we will fight to make this right. Thank you so much 💙 — Team Emily
* * *
**EMILY'S MESSAGE:** Peter — I recorded a message for you right after today’s critical budget vote. [_**If you have Instagram, please take a second to watch and share:**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111257223/556626571/-1001087726?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.07.10_BigBadBill2_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmQ5ZTQ4ZC1iNDVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9KEYG258os9vKZASG5W_vd2GnCYCmdxpgLK1OVd2BWM=&emci=e7793766-0f5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2fd9e48d-b45d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111257224/556626572/-1001087726?hl=en&refcode=RS_EM_FR_2025.07.10_BigBadBill2_Actives&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NzI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZmQ5ZTQ4ZC1iNDVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9KEYG258os9vKZASG5W_vd2GnCYCmdxpgLK1OVd2BWM=&emci=e7793766-0f5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=2fd9e48d-b45d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=23483442) Here’s what’s weighing on me after today’s vote: For the last 30 hours, I’ve joined my fellow Democrats to fight against the Big Bad Bill, the Big Sh**y Bill, the Big Betrayal — whatever you prefer to call the destructive Republican budget. **Trump calls it “beautiful.” But there’s no beauty in this bill. It’s the largest cut to healthcare and the largest transfer of wealth from working people to the wealthiest 1% in American history.** We used every procedural tactic available to us. We offered dozens of amendments. We read your stories on the House floor. Leader Jeffries spoke for more than eight hours — the longest floor speech ever — to delay the final vote. And thousands of working Americans called Congress to speak out against this bill. Many Republicans also spoke out against this bill — and we needed four of them to show John McCain-like bravery, cross party lines, and defeat this budget. In the final vote, the bill passed 218-214, with only two Republicans crossing party lines. Here’s the harsh truth: No matter how long we speak, how many stories we tell, or how much data we share, these bills pass or fail by the number of votes on this floor. **That’s why I’ve “adopted” a swing district, Arizona’s 6th, so that we can replace MAGA footsoldier Juan Ciscomani with mom, veteran, and community leader JoAnna Mendoza.** Rep. Ciscomani was one of the Republicans who vocally opposed the Big Bad Bill — but caved as soon as Trump and GOP leadership applied a little pressure. We must make sure his constituents know that he just sold them out. clean_body: Emily recorded a quick message for you on Friday, right after the vote on the Big Bad Bill — the budget that slashes Medicaid and food assistance for our neighbors and gives the 1% a huge tax break. | Peter — Emily recorded a quick message for you on Friday, right after the vote on the Big Bad Bill — the budget that slashes Medicaid and food assistance for our neighbors and gives the 1% a huge tax break. and read her note below. Together, we will fight to make this right. Thank you so much 💙 — Team Emily EMILY'S MESSAGE: Peter — I recorded a message for you right after today’s critical budget vote. Here’s what’s weighing on me after today’s vote: For the last 30 hours, I’ve joined my fellow Democrats to fight against the Big Bad Bill, the Big Shy Bill, the Big Betrayal — whatever you prefer to call the destructive Republican budget. Trump calls it “beautiful.” But there’s no beauty in this bill. It’s the largest cut to healthcare and the largest transfer of wealth from working people to the wealthiest 1% in American history. We used every procedural tactic available to us. We offered dozens of amendments. We read your stories on the House floor. Leader Jeffries spoke for more than eight hours — the longest floor speech ever — to delay the final vote. And thousands of working Americans called Congress to speak out against this bill. Many Republicans also spoke out against this bill — and we needed four of them to show John McCain-like bravery, cross party lines, and defeat this budget. In the final vote, the bill passed 218-214, with only two Republicans crossing party lines. Here’s the harsh truth: No matter how long we speak, how many stories we tell, or how much data we share, these bills pass or fail by the number of votes on this floor. That’s why I’ve “adopted” a swing district, Arizona’s 6th, so that we can replace MAGA footsoldier Juan Ciscomani with mom, veteran, and community leader JoAnna Mendoza. Rep. Ciscomani was one of the Republicans who vocally opposed the Big Bad Bill — but caved as soon as Trump and GOP leadership applied a little pressure. We must make sure his constituents know that he just sold them out. If you’re able, I’m asking for two things: = to take back the House and undo the damage of the Trump agenda. = If you can’t donate today or want to help another way, . We’ll have opportunities soon to help win races in Washington, Arizona, and across the country. I'm tired, sad, and heartbroken today. name: Grassroots Army
subject: Your Past Doesn't Define You!
body:
I have 2 DUI's on my record!
---
| | | | | |
---
| | | | Your past doesn’t define you.I’ve made mistakes—2 DUIs to be exact.
---
[ ](https://9l762.r.ah.d.sendibm5.com/mk/cl/f/sh/1t6Af4OiGsDg0fV3Ji0XTonBupr9Lp/QN9Mpi8db5tq)
| | | We all fall short. We all make mistakes. I’ve made more than my share, including two DUIs that could’ve ended in tragedy. But by the grace of God, they didn’t. Instead, they became the turning point that brought me to my knees and closer to Him. God doesn’t waste pain. He uses it. He used mine to humble me, to refine me, and to set my feet on a new path. A path of purpose, discipline, and faith. If you’re carrying shame, hear this: your past doesn’t disqualify you. In fact, it may be the very thing God uses to shape your future. He’s not looking for perfect people. He’s looking for willing hearts. So don’t let the enemy chain you to your past. Let God redeem it.Because with Him, every setback is a setup for a comeback.God Bless You,God Bless the State of Michigan,And God Bless These United States!
---

body:

Friend!

name: Jim Marchant
subject: Big Beautiful News for Nevada
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marchant for Nevada
body:
Standing Strong Behind the Big Beautiful Bill
| | | | | [](https://sb6nl6fbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001nj3JHQsN9z9ZvupBhnmsf8yL2I7m2FCGuyYvdURtKR4M8EG4bdAYvZhsBUXVOaNKDZ0zVcq_yYe7oqKdQJ6MBr2jQxY_XA3EQnzvHFd62Ztm6_R7SoERvxPxQdsvyEiD5-6OYW9UYLklKb8q7uQuipZS6Ne7V_GC&c=R-L1uyWiArIYnpqOcI3DQGoV84a9k2GtMlQpKRnFgrVXmzYavSk6xg==&ch=sO_t41EaqxH5qAiasLpWJoy9fPQp_0h33iEta6cmZ2-bjp9S_kxQ5A==)
---
| Peter,
---
President Trump has signed into law the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, a historic step forward for hardworking Americans. This landmark legislation delivers permanent tax relief, cuts wasteful government spending, secures our borders, strengthens energy independence, and makes critical investments in infrastructure. It's a true victory for the people of Nevada and the entire country.
---
|
---
Here's what it means for Nevada and our communities: • More money in your pocket with lower taxes for families and small businesses • Stronger support for workers through expanded deductions for overtime and tip earners, so your hard work pays off • Improved infrastructure funding for roads, bridges, and essential projects to keep Nevada growing and connected • Support for Nevada's businesses and energy sector with reduced regulations and policies that encourage economic growth
I am proud to stand with President Trump and the America First movement. I am fully behind this bill because it delivers real results for Nevada families, workers, and businesses.
This is just the beginning. I'm excited to see what else President Trump and I can accomplish together to put Nevadans first.
---

Jim Marchant Republican Candidate for U.S. Congress - NV01 [JimMarchant.com](https://sb6nl6fbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001nj3JHQsN9z9ZvupBhnmsf8yL2I7m2FCGuyYvdURtKR4M8EG4bdAYvZhsBUXVOaNKDZ0zVcq_yYe7oqKdQJ6MBr2jQxY_XA3EQnzvHFd62Ztm6_R7SoERvxPxQdsvyEiD5-6OYW9UYLmLavS-Tj8VdsdrH2tl5463&c=R-L1uyWiArIYnpqOcI3DQGoV84a9k2GtMlQpKRnFgrVXmzYavSk6xg==&ch=sO_t41EaqxH5qAiasLpWJoy9fPQp_0h33iEta6cmZ2-bjp9S_kxQ5A==)
