name: Ilhan Omar
subject: Republican Rep. calls me a ‘Muslim terrorist’
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20250710-1422-foote&t=1001&akid=1422%2E342411%2EwLi4O9 ]Squad Victory Fund

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250710-movement.pNQAh9&refcode=em20250710-1422&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=1422%2E342411%2EwLi4O9 ]Congressman Rany Fine Responding to Representative Ilhan Omar: 'I'm
sure it is difficult to see us welcome the killer of so manu of your
fellow Muslim terrorists. The only shame is that you serve in Congress'

Peter — Another
day, another outrageous and hateful statement from Republican members of
Congress.

And this certainly isn’t the first time for Rep. Fine.

Before he came to Congress he tweeted a threat at myself and Rashida
Tlaib. He said we should consider leaving before he arrived and
#BombsAway. He suggested on live TV that Gaza should be nuked. He has even
celebrated the death of Palestinian children responding to an image of a
deceased child with “Thanks for the pic!” Even Marjorie Taylor Greene and
Tucker Carlson condemned him for this. And now he is calling me a “Muslim
terrorist.”

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250710-movement.pNQAh9&refcode=em20250710-1422&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=1422%2E342411%2EwLi4O9 ]Peter, this
level of hatred and Islamophobia is completely unacceptable for a member
of Congress. I am asking you to support the Squad Victory Fund today with
a donation in a show of solidarity as I stand up to Randy Fine and the
entire far-right every day in Congress:

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250710-movement.pNQAh9&refcode=em20250710-1422&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1004&akid=1422%2E342411%2EwLi4O9 ]DONATE $3

Let me be absolutely clear: What Rep. Fine tweeted last night is a
thinly-veiled death threat.

Benjamin Netanyahu has directed the mass murder of tens of thousands of
Palestinians. These are innocent civilians who have been sentenced to
either death or an existence of starvation and trauma by the Israeli
military’s brutal assault of the Gaza strip.

By calling them my “fellow Muslim terrorists,” Rep. Fine is telling us
that he doesn’t see any Palestinians as civilians, but rather targets. And
he’s telling us that he thinks me and other Muslims deserve the same fate.

This is the state of much of the Republican party under Trump. Hateful,
racist, violent, and completely devoid of compassion for their fellow
human beings.

If they have any moral fiber left in their bodies, Republicans would call
on Randy Fine to resign. But we all know they won’t, which is why I’m
asking you to help me show that you have my back, and that our movement
will not be intimidated or silenced.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250710-movement.pNQAh9&refcode=em20250710-1422&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1005&akid=1422%2E342411%2EwLi4O9 ]Please, contribute to the Squad Victory Fund today as we work together
to combat Rep. Randy Fine’s violent, racist rhetoric against myself and
millions of Muslim Americans. Your donation helps us prove that our
movement is stronger than ever and ready to go to bat against the MAGA
extremists.

[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/19213?c0=svfem-20250710-movement.pNQAh9&refcode=em20250710-1422&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1024&akid=1422%2E342411%2EwLi4O9 ]Contribute ››

No matter the amount of vitriol and hate they throw my way, I will never
back down.

In solidarity,

Ilhan Omar

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.squadvictoryfund.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1422%2E342411%2EwLi4O9

Paid for by The Squad Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, and Rooted in Community Leadership PAC.

In addition to what’s legally required, The Squad Victory Fund does not accept contributions from: (1) corporations, associations, or their PACs; (2) individuals registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA; (3) from the PACs, lobbyists, or SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies; or (4) from the Fraternal Order of Police.

Contributions or gifts to The Squad Victory Fund are not tax deductible.

Contributions to The Squad Victory Fund will be allocated as follows: 8.33% to each of Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, Rashida Tlaib for Congress, Courage to Change, Inspiring Leadership Has a Name PAC, Power of Us PAC, Rooted in Community Leadership PAC, Politivist PAC, and For the Children PAC.

Any contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if any contributor makes a contribution that, when allocated, would exceed the amount that the contributor may lawfully give to any participant.

After contributing to The Squad Victory Fund, you will automatically be signed up to receive email updates from Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, Ilhan for Congress, The Committee to Elect Ayanna Pressley, and Rashida Tlaib for Congress. You may of course opt-out by unsubscribing at any time.

To contribute via check, please address to The Squad Victory Fund c/o MBA Consulting Group, PO Box 15845, Washington D.C., 20003

Email us: info@squadvictoryfund.com

Right now, **1 in 5 rural counties in America has no functioning local Democratic Party.** _No_ county chair. _No_ local organizing. _No_ path forward. Without party leadership in these communities, Democratic presence vanishes — with real consequences:
* Up to **8% drop in Democratic vote share**
* Up to **10% drop in voter contact**
* **Fewer Democrats running** up and down the ballot.

clean_body: | = | | Hi, Zoë Stein here, Executive Director of Contest Every Race. We recruit and support Democrats in places that have been written off — places where MAGA extremism takes root by default. Right now, 1 in 5 rural counties in America has no functioning local Democratic Party. No county chair. No local organizing. No path forward. Without party leadership in these communities, Democratic presence vanishes — with real consequences: Up to 8% drop in Democratic vote share Up to 10% drop in voter contact Fewer Democrats running up and down the ballot. This is what rural collapse looks like — and we’re the only national program rebuilding Democratic infrastructure in these communities at scale. = | | | = In 2024, we awarded 350 local Democratic teams with $1.2 million in grants, funding year-round work that renewed faith in Democrats , recruited new candidates , and reengaged voters in communities long overlooked. In 2025, we’re stepping up even further. We’ve accepted 390 local teams into our program, more than ever before — but we can only support them with your help. The data couldn’t be clearer: When local Democratic Parties disappear, so do the voters. When we invest in rebuilding, Democrats start winning again. Let’s stop the collapse before it becomes permanent. Will you help us Contest Every Race? Folks, =** \- Zoë Stein; Executive Director, Contest Every Race | | = | | We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in touch with supporters like you. | | Friend — Fifty-six years ago, Georgia Governor Lester Maddox urged state officials to shut down public schools rather than comply with federal integration orders. This was beyond a simple policy disagreement. This was about upholding white power and denying Black students their constitutional right to education. But the former governor and plenty of folks alike preferred to dismantle educational institutions rather than have progress towards racial equity. Today, this fight for educational justice is far from over. What’s happening is a coordinated attack by the Trump Regime and Republicans across the country to advance a far-right agenda that enhances white supremacy and disproportionately impacts Black and brown communities. [**This is exactly why Fair Fight is fighting back — to defend our right to education, voting freedom, and other democratic institutions against attempts to dismantle them. Donate to fuel our efforts.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111266618/556659391/-1427917443?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20250710_T1_OnThisDay_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWYwZWVhZC1jYzVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qh0uRYnCp8HLR_uoJCPXR4gnHYMmTHyqVzDc0YE1n0s=) | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111266619/556659392/-1427917443?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20250710_T1_OnThisDay_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWYwZWVhZC1jYzVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qh0uRYnCp8HLR_uoJCPXR4gnHYMmTHyqVzDc0YE1n0s=)

Right now, we’re seeing:
* Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion rollbacks in higher education
* Nationwide book bans, targeting Black and brown authors and artists
* Efforts to dismantle the Department of Education
* Far-right elected officials on local school boards pushing anti-history, anti-fact agendas

**Even “Our Time Is Now” by Stacey Abrams originally made the list of banned books** from the U.S. Naval Academy library under the Trump Regime—a book that chronicles the erosion of voting rights, highlights the far right’s attacks on democracy, and provides a blueprint for ending voter suppression and empowering our fellow citizens to uphold and expand free and fair elections.The Navy has since walked back its ban of over 250 books.
**Friend: This is exactly why every single election matters.** From local school boards to state legislatures, all the way to the White House, who we elect in every office has the power to shape our livelihoods and every generation after.
Racism, extremism, and the quest for power unite far-right officials at every level of government. They’re dead-set on attacking education, our nation’s honest history, and voting freedom. These attacks are made possible by suppressing the vote and silencing the voices of marginalized communities.
**This is why Fair Fight exists: to protect and expand the freedom to vote, and to ensure that every eligible voter can tap into their power at the ballot box without far-right interference. **
[**With our freedoms under threat, and the Trump Regime and extremists trying to keep us from defending them, we must double down on our fight for democracy. Contribute to advancing Fair Fight’s efforts in ensuring that every voice is heard and empowered.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111266620/556659393/-1427917443?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20250710_T1_OnThisDay_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1YWYwZWVhZC1jYzVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qh0uRYnCp8HLR_uoJCPXR4gnHYMmTHyqVzDc0YE1n0s=)

clean_body: Right here at home in in 1969: | | | Friend — Fifty-six years ago, Georgia Governor Lester Maddox urged state officials to shut down public schools rather than comply with federal integration orders. This was beyond a simple policy disagreement. This was about upholding white power and denying Black students their constitutional right to education. But the former governor and plenty of folks alike preferred to dismantle educational institutions rather than have progress towards racial equity. Today, this fight for educational justice is far from over. What’s happening is a coordinated attack by the Trump Regime and Republicans across the country to advance a far-right agenda that enhances white supremacy and disproportionately impacts Black and brown communities. = | = Right now, we’re seeing: Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion rollbacks in higher education Nationwide book bans, targeting Black and brown authors and artists Efforts to dismantle the Department of Education Far-right elected officials on local school boards pushing anti-history, anti-fact agendas Even “Our Time Is Now” by Stacey Abrams originally made the list of banned books from the U.S. Naval Academy library under the Trump Regime—a book that chronicles the erosion of voting rights, highlights the far right’s attacks on democracy, and provides a blueprint for ending voter suppression and empowering our fellow citizens to uphold and expand free and fair elections.The Navy has since walked back its ban of over 250 books. Friend: This is exactly why every single election matters. From local school boards to state legislatures, all the way to the White House, who we elect in every office has the power to shape our livelihoods and every generation after. Racism, extremism, and the quest for power unite far-right officials at every level of government. They’re dead-set on attacking education, our nation’s honest history, and voting freedom. These attacks are made possible by suppressing the vote and silencing the voices of marginalized communities. This is why Fair Fight exists: to protect and expand the freedom to vote, and to ensure that every eligible voter can tap into their power at the ballot box without far-right interference. = If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will process automatically: = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- = | = ---|--- In solidarity, Fair Fight = | * 1270 Caroline Street | Suite D120-430 | Atlanta, GA 30307 ©2025 Fair Fight - All rights reserved. body:

Fellow Conservative,

I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to.

Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: _**Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss.**_ While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos.

Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away.

I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— _**just weeks after 9/11.**_

_**I didn’t wait. I went.**_

And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map.

I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter.

Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign.
_**

[**HELP ME PROTECT THE AMERICA WE LOVE
CHIP IN $10 TODAY**](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=bdb43bcd2df84f93bc8fb31d42a5dbeb&_e=9_zgD-lJ7tMktfyk9GOvKpJjLaduFPNRh1TPWO0_kEqYmkABJOD_L2Q7pFSGd2IGWp2W8BZbvbI21L3e6N-UDK-hi_0DRWNiE7uOPHPpeQwIJ2aO2qYYr-Z8SwjlhZo71bg3dkqQfKXsZPuhXdWSr_ii0wEVf0S7_k1VZMeUTdmpghdL4gT_wyYkw1uQ-O1kksoeR4Gbpo9WTdqdtz615yGuAW9AuhKlRGrA-zqBKWDZglXAum9PHVDPRFVfIqTpk-GtVbT_K74K7e1pJ1m1kO0FkaUSm-LGXrW8RgM_chgNCHad5WHiCwhfM1J0Kc0lvrTWY1ZV0MoC9bwytP2v9NIPF15p-AEejhONVwFexWBwr6zBYNZ5VxVJd3OOVnnRkEPQu_8grgMjQT88hme2wODRAHEkMJeR1C28yDAtY_I2ZJwRlVqXuW5_C_TXcytNcW93_1_oSR3js3Tp-jl0TwUx22lhl-4Enh6a4Zdm2R9uVUzXV-aFkUdGoFocAFZMTIJ7EtSlD-Hw99zTSYU7fFvU2oCgK9_kb-F-91ig1bxcV64ckvCuSWdQOnmNaojjA5F0vTYPopaJoZJvLQ8HmQAP4KJ_C6VW4yupVHcsJFTtG7GLir9z5yZMJ_7LyGj7X2_Lgr0BMEkDbcrNjSMn6OO_oM5JaAMyup-8HZyHXwqgbORhebUS6KUiV7jK7HliAdhpNufThRVz4p6NpUKh-S0XLJbPKWLGb6v0Ek03SNSNmgkpM86QyvWNABU011Rs51ollmIKTj3uYKFbWCRFdbLZIB53e43rfTECjPOnR5k3yzfkHfGpNHrahmt2uwP7)
**_ **_Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win._**

_ _[](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=bdb43bcd2df84f93bc8fb31d42a5dbeb&_e=9_zgD-lJ7tMktfyk9GOvKpJjLaduFPNRh1TPWO0_kEqYmkABJOD_L2Q7pFSGd2IGjw_0UODvYKz_-PUgxwM76GRv6fO74rzMphe8h4enFtVPBo8Cxxhf9nw2AnKZ2saUWiHL9paVBl0GFSz3jYYF0DFcTjG26s-tur8IppvOjvIZO1PaxKbCYWN4Ubtqo03JUiR57KLj1pcbsBcDyNGhlDGwQtoW8uLeFEJW8hH_LPHgiwMg121kA1vOGThqTS6ocvsBCR0KW3pZxhyNJJq_jC6J4PXoeFUaQs5ScdqHYUW5UBH5f89XWTSqNtsr5Oz3ioSl5bEsJoSFgJS4g_0hYMhvgTwl6R0XSNh-rhJ0b0evE9DdYq5rdnzHYAvC6dXy9TKMwHHsiQWdnMYRntCZODM13UTrFtG2CzW5olkI29AWA479-u3k5krINUvhq6h2SqtoSwIB40jofCAWqpAvjB64Cv3JyTJrdPkosaLL_Q42LIAtEsV1wdzpYAWdOtfPVCIHK3vROg4k1yb1N0hDfjf6yQFi7Ydte5KyJnHhOB8pY2JfZN0qoVoiSHiPKmvB3VmfdhllfK6BXFSNZpvp89vQ7ZkpFMY_rqtFtGUmgfEET-g1bXPSekNpjjsZ6SW5wppgRo7PP1j48W1a-rwAmO5RZDBneSk6G4UhGSJk6OFLy5ra8vk2ME3gCB8LQiS39neVaM1Zi_IceF5lyq07VOvxXjZ5wFOasExoFO93by6U3fUQxia-9lsvD76Uby8MMzAhSNJhlTBgsPX4sTODwQYF_6I9b0rP0n7MxlakbO0%3D)_

This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. **But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for.**

And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for.

We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest.

**If you believe in what I’m fighting for,[even $5 goes a long way.](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=bdb43bcd2df84f93bc8fb31d42a5dbeb&_e=9_zgD-lJ7tMktfyk9GOvKpJjLaduFPNRh1TPWO0_kEqYmkABJOD_L2Q7pFSGd2IGWp2W8BZbvbI21L3e6N-UDK-hi_0DRWNiE7uOPHPpeQwIJ2aO2qYYr-Z8SwjlhZo71bg3dkqQfKXsZPuhXdWSr_ii0wEVf0S7_k1VZMeUTdmpghdL4gT_wyYkw1uQ-O1kksoeR4Gbpo9WTdqdtz615yGuAW9AuhKlRGrA-zqBKWDZglXAum9PHVDPRFVfIqTpk-GtVbT_K74K7e1pJ1m1kO0FkaUSm-LGXrW8RgM_chj8JaoHnD7akkALhv6yH8lRr0i1NW-9s4PAAbTrN5k3NaGzPCHQjjkKYkghGPWioHd0cE10YPPoGu5D5mJoeQLpnrHjg1__OZt4bZceWabTPH18DHlFa5Au4PwUhzPTqTQoXfk5eP52mM3Ih-Q-feyUlI0aldZonnMbov8yTTDaJDn9u-8qszSDzi73fPSwCGXfN94EvszoovZyBEJIua6GNH-5F5fxQStQrOHB_fvEAmaCPUhVMkXA42YptdzKq8I5XQjRQcy5aN5yCo7EhprDHPKWKkiJvvi5mSvp8fOFauhJT1E2wiDj5xRmRv6xdKnGf3QdjR-GwP0PfbVLmJ1pgrqypD_Rmu0rHJ22gCN2bPOmohH22MfWCyLiJ1Rt5h-moNUiJoBXJOfNrtOUIjzJhLfbvkvrJ9R0mhES5RL7H1_ebvIeKgX-ADRHZv73FJ8fUeG5PRMkXokWbP4xQxq6tTPcIlj3JyGXRKKJ8oXlcvVD8r-M9WrfguRoriIVJf62Hyjp6-3ClXm2jyC71MTu)**

If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now.

Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together.

**You’ve done your part for this country.[Let me do mine—will you donate $15 to help?](http://links.freedomfirstalert.com/u/click?_t=9ca143e971904d26a973d002cb0ae7f7&_m=bdb43bcd2df84f93bc8fb31d42a5dbeb&_e=9_zgD-lJ7tMktfyk9GOvKpJjLaduFPNRh1TPWO0_kEqYmkABJOD_L2Q7pFSGd2IGWp2W8BZbvbI21L3e6N-UDK-hi_0DRWNiE7uOPHPpeQwIJ2aO2qYYr-Z8SwjlhZo71bg3dkqQfKXsZPuhXdWSr_ii0wEVf0S7_k1VZMeUTdmpghdL4gT_wyYkw1uQ-O1kksoeR4Gbpo9WTdqdtz615yGuAW9AuhKlRGrA-zqBKWDZglXAum9PHVDPRFVfIqTpk-GtVbT_K74K7e1pJ1m1kO0FkaUSm-LGXrW8RgM_chixLwgemBLdd2Qjt3Uc3jOYkjHsCuYnhzBAgrgzjkmXqNu1a6tC7bHc1Qu9k0sHWs6qqHcMqTiLw0rdsCRk_WqvnwmPHmP4dXmnst1YcBKPQMiWFxkv_bXE7nr7ZAxqC5uodsnWsdw6x80uoDb4qHOWMOUKh6dxZMQOB39TetOF_9RoIXexseixOP7TvvFx99qDzkFUeAG-0R9vOVrYR7Ra80YyPtCsapzzDxqjDd1Qorti6SpwR3jpDZa8Uhn3mLjttMpV1Rki9Exq6UtvxkiAar07b85cAycEZ2j6JTPNzj_OmOsu9O4PHsQsTT_s768P-v0Y9mzstDZm9V3icclsycJRQRwfx9A6hIwOZNfjiAw9rjrXCREhENZlkWgN78MC5d9jOtSKMKFjEgILbRS8L7_gh9RvecWSDL_zAISFQESeUtjsR8CNF9DdtpucFDGujsg9Mc-XdyJTKSx-pfJtqa8-BgZv5OM9Et8ZjmoEo3qZAIqdn5yzdMD4daAsUUePmFZoY9Mfq0wsc6zdkCcB)**

clean_body: I bet this wasn’t on your radar when you woke up this morning | Fellow Conservative, I’m Adam Schwarze—former Navy SEAL Officer, Marine Corps veteran, lifelong Minnesotan, and proud son of two working-class parents. I’ve deployed nine times to 70 countries to serve the country I love. I never expected to run for the U.S. Senate—but looking at what’s happening in our state and nation, I know I have to. Minnesota is being led by two extreme voices: Governor Tim Walz and his lieutenant governor—my opponent—who is somehow even more radical than her boss. While our cities burned in 2020, she led the charge against law enforcement while Walz hesitated. That’s not leadership. That’s chaos. Meanwhile, crime has increased, small businesses have struggled, and our schools fail to teach the basics. The Minnesota I grew up in—where I learned to skate, hunt, and respect my neighbors—feels like it’s slipping away. I joined the Marine Corps on my 18th birthday—October 2001— just weeks after 9/11. I didn’t wait. I went. And for the next 20+ years, I continued to serve. Not behind a desk or on cable news, but in the field—on missions that mattered, often in places you’ll never see on a map. I don’t come from wealth or political pedigree. I come from hard work, long winters, and a deep desire to serve something bigger than myself. I earned degrees from Harvard, Johns Hopkins, and my MBA—not because I wanted to impress anyone, but because I wanted to serve better and lead smarter. Now, I’m taking that same spirit to this campaign. Every dollar helps build a campaign ready to fight and win. This won’t be easy. Minnesota hasn’t voted Republican in a presidential race since 1976, and, unfortunately, hasn’t had Republican representation in the Senate since 2009. But I’ve always thrived in harsh environments and never backed down from a challenge worth fighting for. And truthfully, I’m not running because I dreamed of holding office. I’m running because every time I came home from deployment, things seemed worse—more divided, less free, and further removed from the Minnesota and America I was willing to risk my life for. We need a new kind of leadership rooted in service, not self-interest. If you believe in what I’m fighting for, If you’re not ready to donate today, that’s okay. I just want to earn your trust in the weeks and months ahead. But if you believe—like I do—that Minnesota and America deserve better, please chip in now. Let’s take this fight to the Senate. And let’s do it together. You’ve done your part for this country. In liberty Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota Former Navy SEAL officer Marine Corps Veteran Proud Minnesotan FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. Use of his military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.

name: Andy Beshear
subject: This will hit rural America right in the face
disclaimer_text: Paid for by In This Together PAC
body:
I have been very outspoken about this bill, and for good reason.
Peter,

Last week, Congress passed a “Big, Ugly Bill” that’s a direct attack on rural America. It hands out massive tax breaks to the wealthy while stripping healthcare from millions of Americans and closing rural hospitals. I have been very outspoken about this bill, and for good reason: it will be detrimental to Kentucky and rural America. Here’s just some of what we can expect from this disastrous bill in my state alone:

– 200,000 Kentuckians will be kicked off Medicaid and lose their health insurance
– 35 rural hospitals will close due to loss in funding
– 20,000 healthcare workers will lose their job due to these hospital closures

What’s more? By cutting crucial programs that Kentuckians rely on, like food assistance, Congress is putting the burden on states to cover hundreds of millions of dollars in new costs. Every governor has to balance their state’s budget; no state has hundreds of millions in the couch cushions to pay for these devastating cuts.

_**You don’t have to look very long at these numbers to understand why I’ve fought so hard to push Congress to vote no on this bill.**_

But it’s more than just these numbers that will be impacted by this bill. **Our workforce will become less productive,** as workers will have to take time off to drive hours to the nearest doctor when they are sick. **Our families will become overburdened,** having to become full-time care workers on top of already demanding jobs because they had to take in their sick relatives who lost their care. **Our small businesses will suffer,** as Kentuckians have to pinch pennies even more after losing their job.

This bill is going to hit rural America hard, and every Republican who voted for this bill knows it. I’m calling on these leaders to take a step back, and reexamine the pledge they made when they took the Oath of Office. I want them to be reminded that leadership isn’t defined by how fast you fall in line with your party, but how hard you fight back for the people you were elected to serve.

[**ADD YOUR NAME TODAY: Demand Republican leaders in Congress, and across the country, serve their constituents and not their party. It’s time to end the partnership and do what’s right for the American people >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111266826/556660013/-2141926142?ms=em25_250710_rurala_adv_gq_act___AB_ettx&refcode=em25_250710_rurala_adv_gq_act___AB_ettx&source=em25_250710_rurala_adv_gq_act___AB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250710_rurala_adv_gq_act___AB_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=n/IAXLH0bag0Vpc4uAfmHbrwy5ZpMeSfUP1xthTcqJXALctm7hN/QBmuE4XemWv4Fk2tLsXZUHWzh37P06TpvOkZYo2OeE8qeyGy3PWHLWVnjRbY2UsqWW8TXrbqusuLj+AiJNC36qSA7I48n2i25ItBytjMFbkin+mi+hP9P9YGgjLNdWlfvcTUdMAoBt4w&refcode2=977d5a3e-cd5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzNjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTc3ZDVhM2UtY2Q1ZC1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=mfo8dDDyolKzI2yeY0BwldRsf-Gbp_uuSOGZxcQKEaU=&emci=d405852b-b05d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=977d5a3e-cd5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=17458827) [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111266827/556660014/-2141926142?ms=em25_250710_rurala_adv_gq_act___AB_ettx&refcode=em25_250710_rurala_adv_gq_act___AB_ettx&source=em25_250710_rurala_adv_gq_act___AB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250710_rurala_adv_gq_act___AB_ettx&utm_medium=email&contactdata=n/IAXLH0bag0Vpc4uAfmHbrwy5ZpMeSfUP1xthTcqJXALctm7hN/QBmuE4XemWv4Fk2tLsXZUHWzh37P06TpvOkZYo2OeE8qeyGy3PWHLWVnjRbY2UsqWW8TXrbqusuLj+AiJNC36qSA7I48n2i25ItBytjMFbkin+mi+hP9P9YGgjLNdWlfvcTUdMAoBt4w&refcode2=977d5a3e-cd5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjEvMS8xMTAzNjMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTc3ZDVhM2UtY2Q1ZC1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=mfo8dDDyolKzI2yeY0BwldRsf-Gbp_uuSOGZxcQKEaU=&emci=d405852b-b05d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=977d5a3e-cd5d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=17458827) Thank you for standing with me,

I’m calling on these leaders to take a step back, and reexamine the pledge they made when they took the Oath of Office. I want them to be reminded that leadership isn’t defined by how fast you fall in line with your party, but how hard you fight back for the people you were elected to serve. Thank you for standing with me, Andy | | Andy and In This Together PAC are working to support leaders who will lead with compassion and decency, reject anger politics, and always do what's right. ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/Non-Re-Elect%20Logo.png]
BREAKING : Texas Governor Greg Abbott has announced a special legislative session for later this month—and he added redrawing the state’s Congressional districts to the agenda:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe250709?refcode=lpfe250710z [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe250709?refcode=lpfe250710z]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe250709?refcode=lpfe250710z]
Donald Trump wants Texas Republicans to redraw Texas’ congressional maps—a brazen, unconstitutional power grab aimed at protecting their hold on the House of Representatives and silencing the voices of Texas voters.
No matter what happens, we must be ready. Can you make a contribution today to help us prepare? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe250709?refcode=lpfe250709]
Republicans know that they’re at risk of losing seats in the midterm elections. That’s why they’re trying to rig the system to carve out as many as five new GOP seats in Texas alone. That means eliminating five Democratic seats. But their scheme could backfire. Redistricting can cut both ways, and shifting district lines could end up threatening GOP seats, too.
We have an opportunity to show Donald Trump and Texas Republicans that Texans won’t stand for more partisan gerrymandering and that we’re ready to defend leaders like Lizzie who fight for our communities and our values in Congress.
We can win these battles if we prove that overwhelming support for Lizzie and candidates like her will shrink the Republican majority in Congress—not expand it. But that’s only possible if we have the resources to reach voters, fight back against misinformation, and run strong campaigns and candidates all across the state.
If you’re able, can you make a contribution to help us keep up the fight and stop Donald Trump and House Republicans from rigging the Texas congressional maps in their favor? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpfe250709?refcode=lpfe250710z]
Let’s remind the GOP that you don’t mess with Texas—or with our democracy.
Thanks,
Team Lizzie

name: Sharice Davids
subject: I’m proud of my background. I only wish there were more of us in Congress.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
I wanted to share a few stories (and some pictures too!) about where I come from and why I’m in this fight

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111267100/556661013/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMWVmMTExNS1jZTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YMCP1MLSxlo2iJVX12qLrY5Fj1rPwG3VE1irfi6F-d0=) Hey, I want to be upfront: In just a moment, I’m going to ask you to [_**chip in to my campaign to protect KS-03.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111267101/556661014/-1340363074?refcode=email_071025_story&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMWVmMTExNS1jZTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YMCP1MLSxlo2iJVX12qLrY5Fj1rPwG3VE1irfi6F-d0=) But before I do that, I wanted to share a few stories (and some pictures too!) about where I come from and why I’m in this fight. There is no one in the world more inspiring to me than my mom. She spent more than 20 years serving her country in the Army, all while teaching me the value of hard work and the responsibility all of us have to give back to our communities. I carried those lessons with me when I worked multiple jobs to pay my way through school — and I still carry them with me today. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111267102/556661015/-1340363074?refcode=email_071025_story&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMWVmMTExNS1jZTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YMCP1MLSxlo2iJVX12qLrY5Fj1rPwG3VE1irfi6F-d0=) After I graduated, I went on to work in economic and community development on Native American reservations, helping tribes create programs and initiatives for growth. (Not to mention, a brief stint as an MMA fighter too!) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111267103/556661016/-1340363074?refcode=email_071025_story&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMWVmMTExNS1jZTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YMCP1MLSxlo2iJVX12qLrY5Fj1rPwG3VE1irfi6F-d0=) And in 2018, I ran for Congress in a district that had elected just one Democrat in the last 50 years. I had never run for elected office before, but after I saw Donald Trump and National Republicans ignore their responsibilities to their constituents time and time again, I knew I had to take action. The pundits believed I wouldn’t stand a chance. They thought there was no way a state like Kansas would ever elect a Democrat to Congress, much less an LGBTQ+ Native American woman. But that November, we proved them wrong. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111267104/556661017/-1340363074?refcode=email_071025_story&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMWVmMTExNS1jZTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=YMCP1MLSxlo2iJVX12qLrY5Fj1rPwG3VE1irfi6F-d0=) I’m proud that I have a unique background in Congress. I only wish that there were more of us here. More people who worked their way through school. More people committed to results over rhetoric. More people who weren’t born on third base. clean_body: I wanted to share a few stories (and some pictures too!) about where I come from and why I’m in this fight = Hey, I want to be upfront: In just a moment, I’m going to ask you to = But before I do that, I wanted to share a few stories (and some pictures too!) about where I come from and why I’m in this fight. There is no one in the world more inspiring to me than my mom. She spent more than 20 years serving her country in the Army, all while teaching me the value of hard work and the responsibility all of us have to give back to our communities. I carried those lessons with me when I worked multiple jobs to pay my way through school — and I still carry them with me today. = After I graduated, I went on to work in economic and community development on Native American reservations, helping tribes create programs and initiatives for growth. (Not to mention, a brief stint as an MMA fighter too!) = And in 2018, I ran for Congress in a district that had elected just one Democrat in the last 50 years. I had never run for elected office before, but after I saw Donald Trump and National Republicans ignore their responsibilities to their constituents time and time again, I knew I had to take action. The pundits believed I wouldn’t stand a chance. They thought there was no way a state like Kansas would ever elect a Democrat to Congress, much less an LGBTQ+ Native American woman. But that November, we proved them wrong. = I’m proud that I have a unique background in Congress. I only wish that there were more of us here. More people who worked their way through school. More people committed to results over rhetoric. More people who weren’t born on third base. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = With gratitude, Sharice | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. When thousands of Democrats step up together, even with just a few bucks, we can’t be ignored. Your $3 helps Speak Up PAC support candidates who are committed to fighting for democracy, like the Democratic Party of Georgia. Your $3 helps fund groups putting out hard-hitting ads, organizing in battleground states, and turning out the voters who decide elections, like the Democratic Party of Georgia. Your $3 helps us take on Trump’s lies, expose his allies, and flip the seats that will decide control of Congress. If just 7,500 supporters chipped in $3 before midnight, we’ll hit our $60,000 goal – and keep this important fight going strong. name: Deb Haaland
subject: From my kitchen to yours: green chile chicken posole
body:
Cooking has always been a way for me to foster connection and build community. |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111267428/556662095/-847979181?refcode=WUEFH718_20250710_HaalandPosoleSplit_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNDhhYzk0Yi1jZjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dv-2GYy6wL75jRlZOBUjoX2MOtePRlMGuwasjkEfTR8=) Hey Peter – it's Deb Haaland. I'm running for governor of New Mexico and EMILYs List said I could reach out to you.

One thing you should know about me is that **I love to cook**.

My love of cooking New Mexican cuisine started early, thanks to the hours I spent watching my grandmother bake bread in her mud oven and stir huge pots of posole. She wouldn't let us kids in the kitchen while she cooked, but I'd perch myself outside and watch her through the window for hours. I still hold those memories close as I prepare my favorite traditional New Mexican dishes. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111267429/556662096/-847979181?refcode=WUEFH718_20250710_HaalandPosoleSplit_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNDhhYzk0Yi1jZjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dv-2GYy6wL75jRlZOBUjoX2MOtePRlMGuwasjkEfTR8=) _New Mexico chiles – the best in the world!_ There's something powerful about connecting with others over a home-cooked meal. When I was in law school at UNM, I'd make chicken green chile stew for student gatherings, and I made that same dish for my congressional colleagues during the 2019 winter holiday season. I even cooked chile and tortillas for the water protectors when I traveled to Standing Rock during the protests against the Dakota Access Pipeline.

Cooking became more than a hobby after I decided to start my own salsa business. I worked out of a commercial kitchen and bought my red chile pequin from a farm in Northern New Mexico. I'd prepare my salsa and deliver jars to mom-and-pop shops across the state, often with Somáh riding with me in the back seat. [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111267430/556662101/-847979181?refcode=WUEFH718_20250710_HaalandPosoleSplit_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNDhhYzk0Yi1jZjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dv-2GYy6wL75jRlZOBUjoX2MOtePRlMGuwasjkEfTR8=) _Here's Somáh posing with jars of my homemade salsa. Kids in New Mexico start early... 🌶️_ **For me, cooking has always been a way to foster connection and build community, which is exactly what we're doing now as we grow our campaign for New Mexico.** I've had the chance to sit down with New Mexicans in living rooms and at kitchen tables to hear about their lives, their struggles, and their hopes for the future. Every conversation is another reminder of the incredibly high stakes in this race – and proof that if we organize around our shared values, we will win. [**Will you split a donation between my campaign and EMILYs List today? If you donate through this link, I'll even send you my recipe for green chile chicken posole!**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111267431/556662106/-847979181?refcode=WUEFH718_20250710_HaalandPosoleSplit_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNDhhYzk0Yi1jZjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dv-2GYy6wL75jRlZOBUjoX2MOtePRlMGuwasjkEfTR8=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $15](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111267432/556662112/-847979181?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=WUEFH718_20250710_HaalandPosoleSplit_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNDhhYzk0Yi1jZjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dv-2GYy6wL75jRlZOBUjoX2MOtePRlMGuwasjkEfTR8=) | [DONATE $35](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111267433/556662116/-847979181?express_lane=true&amount=35&refcode=WUEFH718_20250710_HaalandPosoleSplit_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056560&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNDhhYzk0Yi1jZjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dv-2GYy6wL75jRlZOBUjoX2MOtePRlMGuwasjkEfTR8=)

clean_body: Cooking has always been a way for me to foster connection and build community. | = Hey Peter – it's Deb Haaland. I'm running for governor of New Mexico and EMILYs List said I could reach out to you. One thing you should know about me is that I love to cook. My love of cooking New Mexican cuisine started early, thanks to the hours I spent watching my grandmother bake bread in her mud oven and stir huge pots of posole. She wouldn't let us kids in the kitchen while she cooked, but I'd perch myself outside and watch her through the window for hours. I still hold those memories close as I prepare my favorite traditional New Mexican dishes. = New Mexico chiles – the best in the world! There's something powerful about connecting with others over a home-cooked meal. When I was in law school at UNM, I'd make chicken green chile stew for student gatherings, and I made that same dish for my congressional colleagues during the 2019 winter holiday season. I even cooked chile and tortillas for the water protectors when I traveled to Standing Rock during the protests against the Dakota Access Pipeline. Cooking became more than a hobby after I decided to start my own salsa business. I worked out of a commercial kitchen and bought my red chile pequin from a farm in Northern New Mexico. I'd prepare my salsa and deliver jars to mom-and-pop shops across the state, often with Somáh riding with me in the back seat. = Here's Somáh posing with jars of my homemade salsa. Kids in New Mexico start early... 🌶️ For me, cooking has always been a way to foster connection and build community, which is exactly what we're doing now as we grow our campaign for New Mexico. I've had the chance to sit down with New Mexicans in living rooms and at kitchen tables to hear about their lives, their struggles, and their hopes for the future. Every conversation is another reminder of the incredibly high stakes in this race – and proof that if we organize around our shared values, we will win. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Happy cooking, Deb 🌶️ = = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: Eric Swalwell
subject: I’m calling for Kristi Noem’s resignation
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Seth Moulton for Congress
body:
| Friend, I’m leading the call for Kristi Noem’s resignation. As the **senior-most Democrat on the House Homeland Security Committee** , I’ve seen a lot — but what Noem is doing at the Department of Homeland Security is beyond the pale. She deported a _4-year-old child battling cancer_. She detained a U.S. Marshal. She lied to Congress about Trump’s doctored photo of Kilmar Abrego Garcia. And she’s arrested one member of Congress and detained another just for speaking out against MAGA’s authoritarian regime. **Under her leadership, ICE agents are running around this country terrorizing families, masking their identities, and abusing their power at Noem’s behest.** **I’m demanding accountability — and her resignation. But I need your help.** [**While I fight to ensure Kristi Noem answers for her disregard for the rule of law on the Homeland Security Committee, will you split a contribution between me and Seth Moulton? I’m about to be in the middle of two high stakes investigations against Trump and his henchmen, so I need to be prepared to fight back no matter what they throw my way.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111267373/556661893/265156787?refcode=250710_MFC_Swalwell_Noem&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YjkxMWZmMy1jZDVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=apWPfkLiYp2ZH5_eFZ_WweaPFP-DUOHjo-BfMzAVDOA=) [Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111267374/556661894/-288956247?refcode=250710_MFC_Swalwell_Noem&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YjkxMWZmMy1jZDVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=apWPfkLiYp2ZH5_eFZ_WweaPFP-DUOHjo-BfMzAVDOA=) Democracy dies when we remain silent, friend. name: Mazie Hirono
subject: 1,000 pages of pain
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii**
body:
|
#
---
Peter, On the Fourth of July, Donald Trump and Republicans finalized their betrayal of the American people. Trump signed his Big Ugly Bill into law that rips health care away from Americans, slashes food assistance, weakens public education, and so much more — just to give tax cuts to the richest Americans. Regardless of how many times they call their tax bill “beautiful,” there’s nothing beautiful about what they just did. **This bill is nothing more than 1,000 pages of pain for millions of people in Hawaii and across the country. ** Republicans have proven that they don’t care one bit about how their actions affect anyone who isn’t a billionaire, but I do. That’s why, over the coming weeks, I’ll be working to mitigate the damage the GOP’s betrayal of working families will do to my constituents by meeting with state and local officials, coordinating with community partners, and working with service providers to blunt the harm the GOP’s bill will do to the people of Hawaii. **[I’m also determined to continue my fight against the GOP’s extreme agenda for as long as it takes and elect more Democrats to the Senate who will put people like you first. name: MGP for Congress
subject: Welcome our new team members!
body:
| | Peter –

Marie is facing another tough re-election battle, so we’re working hard to build an unbeatable grassroots campaign that has what it takes to defend this seat once again next year. We just welcomed our three new Democracy Summer Fellows to Team Marie, and we couldn’t be more excited to have them on board to help shore up our campaign outreach right away.

Let us introduce Max, Miley, and Madeleine:
> **Max Robben** , entering his 3rd year at Grinnell College, is greatly excited to be spending his summer working to re-elect MGP. When he's done working for the day, he likes to play board games and spend time with friends.
>
> **Miley Coté** is a rising Junior at the Vancouver School of Arts and Academics. Miley was inspired to spend their summer with Democracy Summer and MGP’s campaign while volunteering for the congresswoman. During their free time, they enjoy playing soccer and taking their dog for long walks.
>
> **Madeleine Schreiber** , a second year at the University of Chicago studying Political Science and Economics, is super excited to be interning with the MGP campaign this summer. In addition to working with MGP, Madeleine also interns with the Counter Extremism Project and works for Ben & Jerry’s Ice Cream.
**In a nationally-watched race that will decide the next House majority, early preparation is key. From welcoming our Fellows this summer, to staffing up and growing our volunteer program over the coming months, this will take serious resources.** [Can you pitch in $10 to help us continue growing our team as we map out the path to Election Day?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111268031/556664179/-618465495?amount=10&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_90DO_250710_1_1&refcode2=8f84560a-d15d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4Zjg0NTYwYS1kMTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2Gsn-0kH2w27pkFloie0-8HZGuJ5gSmoR1AtZ6ErhZ4=) _If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately._ | | **[DONATE $10 NOW »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111268032/556664180/-618465495?amount=10&express_lane=true&refcode=MGP_EM_FR_90DO_250710_1_1&refcode2=8f84560a-d15d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTkyOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4Zjg0NTYwYS1kMTVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2Gsn-0kH2w27pkFloie0-8HZGuJ5gSmoR1AtZ6ErhZ4=)**

clean_body: | | Peter – Marie is facing another tough re-election battle, so we’re working hard to build an unbeatable grassroots campaign that has what it takes to defend this seat once again next year. We just welcomed our three new Democracy Summer Fellows to Team Marie, and we couldn’t be more excited to have them on board to help shore up our campaign outreach right away. Let us introduce Max, Miley, and Madeleine: Max Robben , entering his 3rd year at Grinnell College, is greatly excited to be spending his summer working to re-elect MGP. When he's done working for the day, he likes to play board games and spend time with friends. Miley Coté is a rising Junior at the Vancouver School of Arts and Academics. Miley was inspired to spend their summer with Democracy Summer and MGP’s campaign while volunteering for the congresswoman. During their free time, they enjoy playing soccer and taking their dog for long walks. Madeleine Schreiber , a second year at the University of Chicago studying Political Science and Economics, is super excited to be interning with the MGP campaign this summer. In addition to working with MGP, Madeleine also interns with the Counter Extremism Project and works for Ben & Jerry’s Ice Cream. In a nationally-watched race that will decide the next House majority, early preparation is key. From welcoming our Fellows this summer, to staffing up and growing our volunteer program over the coming months, this will take serious resources. = If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | = | = | = | = | = | = | | | = | Marie Gluesenkamp Perez is a fifth-generation Washingtonian, a small business owner and a pro-choice mother. As the representative for Washington’s third Congressional District, she's working to bring the voices of working people back to DC. name: Voter Action Project
subject: Help us say no to these nominees
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2?am=25.00&contactdata=4hncXg/++RIdn88RTLQHYp7sz7hVgV3/hLRImY2FWR53AsGpiYSKKb9HjwQW9YkKgzoGsBpGPU8RcispNxxTmBmVco7iqVjjEaAYUtuNeb8=]
Folks,
We've reached the next stage in our fight to protect the U.S. Postal Service from privatization. Donald Trump has nominated Anthony Lomangino and John LaValle to the USPS Board of Governors, two very wealthy Wall Street types with deep ties to Trump, zero experience with the Postal Service, and clear records of political loyalty.
Lomangino is a Mar-a-Lago insider and Trump megadonor with deep business ties to the new Postmaster General. Lomangino’s company donated nearly $9 million to a pro-Trump super PAC, and he once gave $150,000 to defend Trump associates under federal investigation. John LaValle is a longtime GOP operative, a former GOP party boss and MAGA surrogate whose loyalty to Trump goes all the way back to 2016.
These men aren’t public servants, and they don’t know the first thing about running the post office. But they are part of a deliberate scheme to dismantle public oversight from the inside in order to fast-track USPS privatization, which has been Trump’s goal all along.
It's simple: A vote to confirm these men is a vote to gut the post office .
Demand your senators reject Trump’s USPS Board nominees and protect the Post Office from privatization now! >> [https://actionnetwork.org/letters/reject-trumps-usps-board-nominees?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://actionnetwork.org/letters/reject-trumps-usps-board-nominees?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]
The USPS has long been a lifeline for millions , especially those in rural, Indigenous, and under-served communities. The postal board must remain independent and focused on public service, not privatization.
Confirming these men would shift the balance of power on the USPS Board and put the future of the postal service in the hands of Trump’s political allies. If they join the board, the makeup would be: 1 Independent, 2 Democrats, 4 Republicans, and 2 open seats.
This is the next step in Trump’s larger plan to dismantle the USPS and hand it over to corporate profiteers. But with your help, we can stop it.
Add your name today to let your Senators know you oppose these nominees and Trump's efforts to privatize the USPS >> [https://actionnetwork.org/letters/reject-trumps-usps-board-nominees?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.everyaction.com%2F9bivrSvjFEim0QMMvgcQrA2]

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.everyaction.com/Qyv_-_BiF0iduOUt5sZ0yw2]

body:
Most of us know or love someone who has struggled with mental illnesses or substance abuse.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111268690/556666754/-1080040549?refcode=email-header&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNGU3MGRmZi1kMjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JbVk28O3-_odxdb0P5siVYlQqcMiLbl56acq_mOYD-M=) Team, Republicans may have rammed through their disastrous and cruel budget bill through Congress, but when I lose a fight, I don’t pack up and go home. There’s still plenty of work to get done, and **I’m not done fighting to deliver real change for Oregon’s working families.** **[I want to tell you a little more about what I’ve been working on in Congress, but if you don’t have time, please consider making a donation today and help keep me in the fight for you and your family. →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111268691/556666755/-480163815?refcode=email_071025_mentalhealth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNGU3MGRmZi1kMjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JbVk28O3-_odxdb0P5siVYlQqcMiLbl56acq_mOYD-M=)** | | **[DONATE →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111268692/556666756/-480163815?refcode=email_071025_mentalhealth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNGU3MGRmZi1kMjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JbVk28O3-_odxdb0P5siVYlQqcMiLbl56acq_mOYD-M=)**

Most of us know or love someone who has struggled with mental illnesses or substance abuse.
For me, it was my sister. Growing up, I watched her struggle with depression, and saw firsthand how easily it can be swept under the rug or outright stigmatized. It’s a serious problem, and far too often goes unaddressed.
**So I decided to step up, and do something about it.**
That’s why I’m proud to have launched the Mental Health Caucus in the House to find commonsense solutions that will help raise awareness, reduce stigma, and increase affordability and access to mental health services across the U.S.
It’s why we introduced a resolution to designate the month of May as Mental Health Awareness Month, and reintroduced our bill to create a new scholarship program for students studying in behavioural health programs.
**And it’s yet another reason why I’ve been so forceful in my opposition to Trump’s Republican Tax Scam that guts Medicaid — _the single largest payer for mental health services in the nation_ — just to pay for a massive tax handout to billionaires.**
We need to get serious about tackling this challenge, because mental health is an issue that touches _all_ of us, regardless of party, race, or class. **[If you agree, please chip in $10 or whatever you can spare and help me keep fighting to put an end to America’s mental health crisis — once and for all. →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111268693/556666757/-480163815?refcode=email_071025_mentalhealth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNGU3MGRmZi1kMjVkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JbVk28O3-_odxdb0P5siVYlQqcMiLbl56acq_mOYD-M=)**

name: NDRC Team
subject: The long history of attacks on the Voting Rights Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
We can’t allow this to happen
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111269213/556668642/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ1ZjE0ZjExLWQ1NWQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=rhOA0NXmFnNa6lOoDENrBpQIzBHSi1w6XVYz3sjzdnI=&emci=842a8415-a45d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&emdi=45f14f11-d55d-f011-8f7c-6045bda9d96b&ceid=8431045)
---

Pop quiz, Peter**—** How do we know that the Voting Rights Act (VRA) matters so much for our democracy? _Because Republicans and partisan judges keep doing everything possible to attack it._ Since the overwhelmingly bipartisan reauthorization of the VRA by Congress in the early 2000s, Republican policymakers and conservatives on the U.S. Supreme Court have consistently sought to chip away at its protections. A series of cases and decisions have put the VRA to the test — often with disastrous consequences for voting rights and fair representation. **We’ll quickly explain this recent history of attacks by the far right in a moment —[ but first, can you make a donation to the NDRC to make sure we can keep fighting gerrymandering and supporting fair representation in states across the country?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/111269214/556668643/-246980373?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_VRAHistory_email_20250710-NDRC-Tier1-VRAHistory_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250710-NDRC-Tier1-VRAHistory_&utm_campaign=VRAHistory&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjQ1ZjE0ZjExLWQ1NWQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGE5ZDk2YiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=rhOA0NXmFnNa6lOoDENrBpQIzBHSi1w6XVYz3sjzdnI=)** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

Here’s a quick rundown of some of the most important challenges to the VRA, and how they have shaped the current voting landscape across the U.S.
**In 2013, the court’s disastrous Shelby County decision struck down a key VRA provision that had been in place since 1965,** which mandated jurisdictions with a record of voter discrimination get federal approval before changing their election laws or redistricting plans. After the ruling, states immediately began attempting to restrict access to the ballot. The Texas Attorney General announced on _the day of the decision_ that his state would enact “the most restrictive voter ID law in the nation,” and in the very next redistricting cycle, Republicans deliberately manipulated maps to dilute Black voting power.
**In 2021, the Supreme Court’s decision in _Brnovich v. DNC_ upheld two racially discriminatory voting rules in Arizona — weakening the VRA and giving states the green light to push through more discriminatory voting practices.** This decision was yet another step in the coordinated assault on our democracy being carried out in the name of “election security” — based on the decades-long lie of widespread election fraud. The true impact, however, is the systematic disenfranchisement of countless American citizens, especially people of color.
**In 2022, the court issued a historic gerrymandering decision — and this time, they were on the _right side_ of democracy and fair representation.** In _Allen v. Milligan_ , the court rejected Alabama Republicans’ brazen attempt to crack Black voters across multiple districts. The manipulated map had resulted in just one of the state’s seven congressional seats truly offering Black voters the chance to elect a candidate of their choice — in a state where 27% of the population is Black. But the U.S. Supreme Court agreed that this violated the VRA and reaffirmed a longstanding legal test for evaluating claims of discrimination in electoral maps.
Rather than comply, Alabama created a new map that was _still discriminatory._ The fair maps movement supported Alabama voters who challenged the latest gerrymander in court, where it was rejected once again — ultimately leading to a new map that complies with the VRA and gave Black voters the opportunity to elect a candidate of their choice in two congressional districts for the 2024 election. Still, the state of Alabama continued to fight enforcement of the VRA, leading to a key court decision in May confirming that its congressional map must have two Black opportunity districts for the remainder of the decade.
**And in 2025 — that’s _this year_ — Republicans have continued to use the courts to attempt to erase Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act** as part of a larger strategy to gerrymander the congressional map ahead of the 2026 midterms. From Alabama to Georgia to Louisiana, ongoing lawsuits could determine whether or not the national congressional map remains the fairest map we have seen in decades or awards Republicans an unfair advantage by dismantling Black opportunity districts.
**Peter, it’s clear as day: Republicans will never stop trying to undermine the VRA and gerrymander their way into power. So we must never stop working to fight back against their attacks.**

clean_body: We can’t allow this to happen | Pop quiz, Peter— How do we know that the Voting Rights Act (VRA) matters so much for our democracy? Because Republicans and partisan judges keep doing everything possible to attack it. Since the overwhelmingly bipartisan reauthorization of the VRA by Congress in the early 2000s, Republican policymakers and conservatives on the U.S. Supreme Court have consistently sought to chip away at its protections. A series of cases and decisions have put the VRA to the test — often with disastrous consequences for voting rights and fair representation. We’ll quickly explain this recent history of attacks by the far right in a moment —= | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Here’s a quick rundown of some of the most important challenges to the VRA, and how they have shaped the current voting landscape across the U.S. In 2013, the court’s disastrous Shelby County decision struck down a key VRA provision that had been in place since 1965, which mandated jurisdictions with a record of voter discrimination get federal approval before changing their election laws or redistricting plans. After the ruling, states immediately began attempting to restrict access to the ballot. The Texas Attorney General announced on the day of the decision that his state would enact “the most restrictive voter ID law in the nation,” and in the very next redistricting cycle, Republicans deliberately manipulated maps to dilute Black voting power. In 2021, the Supreme Court’s decision in Brnovich v. DNC upheld two racially discriminatory voting rules in Arizona — weakening the VRA and giving states the green light to push through more discriminatory voting practices. This decision was yet another step in the coordinated assault on our democracy being carried out in the name of “election security” — based on the decades-long lie of widespread election fraud. The true impact, however, is the systematic disenfranchisement of countless American citizens, especially people of color. In 2022, the court issued a historic gerrymandering decision — and this time, they were on the right side of democracy and fair representation. In Allen v. Milligan , the court rejected Alabama Republicans’ brazen attempt to crack Black voters across multiple districts. The manipulated map had resulted in just one of the state’s seven congressional seats truly offering Black voters the chance to elect a candidate of their choice — in a state where 27% of the population is Black. But the U.S. Supreme Court agreed that this violated the VRA and reaffirmed a longstanding legal test for evaluating claims of discrimination in electoral maps. Rather than comply, Alabama created a new map that was still discriminatory. The fair maps movement supported Alabama voters who challenged the latest gerrymander in court, where it was rejected once again — ultimately leading to a new map that complies with the VRA and gave Black voters the opportunity to elect a candidate of their choice in two congressional districts for the 2024 election. Still, the state of Alabama continued to fight enforcement of the VRA, leading to a key court decision in May confirming that its congressional map must have two Black opportunity districts for the remainder of the decade. And in 2025 — that’s this year — Republicans have continued to use the courts to attempt to erase Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act as part of a larger strategy to gerrymander the congressional map ahead of the 2026 midterms. From Alabama to Georgia to Louisiana, ongoing lawsuits could determine whether or not the national congressional map remains the fairest map we have seen in decades or awards Republicans an unfair advantage by dismantling Black opportunity districts. Peter, it’s clear as day: Republicans will never stop trying to undermine the VRA and gerrymander their way into power. So we must never stop working to fight back against their attacks. = As Attorney General Eric Holder has said: “To this day, the Voting Rights Act remains the most effective law in the country to protect the right to vote... Yet, anti-democratic forces are just as determined today as they were during the Jim Crow era to dismantle these protections in order to suppress the votes of American citizens.” Thanks for being in this fight with us, Team NDRC = = Since 2017, the NDRC has executed a comprehensive redistricting strategy that shifts the redistricting power, creating fair districts where Democrats can compete. Our victories have been made possible by our strong community of grassroots supporters. = If you want to receive periodic updates from the NDRC on our fight for fair maps, text MAPS to 36787. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. We know we send a lot of emails, but we think it's important to keep you updated with the latest information on Republican gerrymandering and voter suppression and how we're fighting back -- regardless of whether you choose to donate. = to make a donation. Every dollar you give will go toward our fight to end gerrymandering and voter suppression. | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC.

**“Kennedy Withdraws U.S. Funding Pledge to International Vaccine Agency”
_\- New York Times_**
* * *
Peter –

Recently, Kim stood up to Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in a committee hearing and denounced his anti-vaccine agenda. She did so because across our country, we’re seeing cases of measles and other eradicated diseases on the rise.

These are cases that could have been prevented. Yet, more and more parents are choosing to not vaccinate their children because of the anti-vaccine propaganda RFK Jr. is pushing.

To make matters worse, RFK is now pulling funds that the United States pledged to the international vaccine agency Gavi. _**Here’s why this should concern you:**_

Gavi helps distribute vaccines to hard to reach places, and is responsible for inoculating 1.1 billion children since its inception in 2000. By helping expand the number of children vaccinated across the globe, we are protecting our own citizens from becoming ill from travel or interaction with citizens in other countries. That protection is now under threat.

clean_body: Vaccines save lives. “Kennedy Withdraws U.S. Funding Pledge to International Vaccine Agency” \- New York Times Peter – Recently, Kim stood up to Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in a committee hearing and denounced his anti-vaccine agenda. She did so because across our country, we’re seeing cases of measles and other eradicated diseases on the rise. These are cases that could have been prevented. Yet, more and more parents are choosing to not vaccinate their children because of the anti-vaccine propaganda RFK Jr. is pushing. To make matters worse, RFK is now pulling funds that the United States pledged to the international vaccine agency Gavi. Here’s why this should concern you: Gavi helps distribute vaccines to hard to reach places, and is responsible for inoculating 1.1 billion children since its inception in 2000. By helping expand the number of children vaccinated across the globe, we are protecting our own citizens from becoming ill from travel or interaction with citizens in other countries. That protection is now under threat. America is losing its footing on being a leader in science and health, simply because Trump hired someone to lead the HHS who believes in conspiracy theories. We’re lucky to live at a time when many Americans don't remember what it was like before we had vaccines. We can’t go back to these dark times. Vaccines save lives. We have the science, so let's use it to protect our children. Thanks for standing with us, Team Schrier Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. name: Mark Kelly
subject: Dark money is pouring into this special election.
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
#

Friends,
In just a few days, there's an important special election for U.S. Congress in Arizona's 7th District. This is my home district, so it's important to me that we elect someone ready to lead — especially after Republicans just passed their so-called "Big Beautiful Bill."
Before I tell you why Adelita Grijalva is the kind of leader we need in Congress, I need to share something urgent. Her campaign is facing a wave of last-minute dark money attacks from out-of-state groups trying to distort her record. **Adelita’s team needs immediate resources to fight back, and they’re counting on grassroots supporters to step up.**
[**Friends, that's why I'm asking personally: Will you rush a contribution directly to Adelita Grijalva's campaign for Congress today? With the election just days away, every dollar truly matters.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-grijalva-split.mvHf9G&refcode=em250710-1-90&t=1&akid=6118%2E6688874%2E2zKykH)
I know Adelita. She grew up here in Southern Arizona, went to public school here, and has dedicated her career to helping Tucson kids and working families get ahead. She's tough, principled, and deeply rooted in the community she serves.
From the school board to the county supervisor's office, Adelita has delivered real results. She's fought to keep housing affordable, raised wages for workers, and led the creation of Southern Arizona's first free preschool program for low-income families.
With Trump’s “Big Beautiful Bill” slashing healthcare, education, and environmental protections, it’s clearer than ever why we need leaders like Adelita in Congress to fight back and put Arizona families first.
Adelita has that kind of political courage. That's why special interests are trying to stop her — because they know she'll stand up to them and fight for working people.
**Let's make sure she has what she needs to win this race.**
[**Rush a contribution to Adelita Grijalva’s campaign today. Every dollar helps her fight back, get her message out, and win this race.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-grijalva-split.mvHf9G&refcode=em250710-1-90&t=2&akid=6118%2E6688874%2E2zKykH)

Peter, I’m fed up.

My Republican colleagues' continue to take actions that hurt communities like NY-15 the hardest.

**From slashing Medicaid to freezing federal funds for essential services, it’s clear that Trump and MAGA Republicans do _not_ have our backs.**

Their irresponsible and undemocratic actions will line the pockets of billionaires, but the hardworking people of NY-15 — and communities like it — will pay the price.

Here’s the thing, Peter: **It doesn’t have to be like this.** But we have to __fight like hell__ to flip the House and change course before Trump and MAGA Republicans completely pull the rug out from under communities like ours.

**_That’s why I need to know if you’re with me, Peter._**

www.torres.nyc

Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. At age 25, Ritchie made history as the youngest elected official to serve on the New York City Council. He fought tirelessly for the working families of The Bronx, and now, he's fighting for those families and for progressive values in Congress. He is the first openly gay Afro Latino ever elected to Congress. Ritchie is a champion for progressive policies to fight poverty and to improve the lives of families just like the one he was raised in.

clean_body: | = Peter, I’m fed up. My Republican colleagues' continue to take actions that hurt communities like NY-15 the hardest. From slashing Medicaid to freezing federal funds for essential services, it’s clear that Trump and MAGA Republicans do not have our backs. Their irresponsible and undemocratic actions will line the pockets of billionaires, but the hardworking people of NY-15 — and communities like it — will pay the price. Here’s the thing, Peter: It doesn’t have to be like this. But we have to fight like hell to flip the House and change course before Trump and MAGA Republicans completely pull the rug out from under communities like ours. That’s why I need to know if you’re with me, Peter. = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = I’m so grateful for your support. Together, Ritchie | | Paid for by Torres For Congress PO Box 580303, Bronx, NY 10458 www.torres.nyc Ritchie Torres represents the future of the Democratic Party. Raised by a single mother in public housing, Ritchie personally understands the hardships that working families endure. name: Team Bennet
subject: We have a new Republican opponent in this race.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250710_gopopponent_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250710_gopopponent_act]
Hi team,
We have a big campaign update: A new Republican opponent has entered our race.
And while you’ve probably never heard of Will McBride, he’s trying to change that with his personal fortune.
He previously (and unsuccessfully ) ran for the U.S. Senate in Florida, where he loaned his own campaign over $700,000.
When he considered running again in 2012, it was reported that he would be able to self-finance his run with $4 million .
Well, Will McBride must be new to Colorado, because here, our elections aren’t bought and sold—they’re earned vote by vote.
Which is precisely the work Michael is doing in this race. He’s reaching out, engaging with, and listening to the concerns of Coloradans. He’s traveling to all four corners of our state, connecting with voters, and telling them about his record of fighting for us—first as the Superintendent of Denver Public Schools, then as our U.S. Senator.
This race is about the people of Colorado, not the donors with the deepest pockets. Michael does not accept donations from corporations or corporate PACs. He needs your grassroots help to fight back against self-funded candidates and other Republicans who will try to push Trump's agenda here in Colorado.
While others rely on big bank accounts or big checks from corporate PACs to fund their campaigns, we rely on small-dollar donors like yourself. Friend, could you help power our campaign by chipping in $10 or $5 today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_mfb_email-25q3?refcode=X_250710_gopopponent_act&amount=10]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Thank you,
Team Bennet
